title: DevOps Engineer
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale transformation programme and are seeking a DevOps / QA Engineer to support the delivery of resilient CI/CD infrastructure, automated testing, and deployment reliability across critical enterprise platforms. This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: Electrical & Instrumentation Apprentice
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Electrical \& Instrumentation Apprentice** **Location:** **Blanchardstown, Co Dublin** **Hours:** **39 Hours per week** The Electrical Instrumentation craftsperson is concerned with the installation, commissioning, testing and maintenance of: \- electrical wiring systems and services; electrical plant and control equipment; process monitoring and control systems. Modern process plant includes electrical, electro\-mechanical, electro\-pneumatic, electronic and microprocessor based systems. In order to function effectively and efficiently, the Electrical/Instrumentation craftsperson must have a broad base of technical knowledge complementary to information gathering and analytical skills. The ability to interpret technical data and the proper use of test instruments is critical for effective system maintenance, fault diagnosis and rectification and the installation/calibration of sensors, transmitting and controlling devices. This craftsperson must maintain effective communication with colleagues, clients and equipment suppliers. Proper maintenance, and updating of job\- related and equipment\-related documentation is critical. All work undertaken by Electrical Instrumentation craftspersons is subject to statutory regulations governing the safety of personnel, plant, premises and the environment. At the end of the apprenticeship, the craftsperson will be able to demonstrate competence in the following skills: **Core Skills** * Selection, procurement, safe use of, and storage of craft\-related tools, equipment and materials * Selection and utilisation of fixing devices * Assessment, interpretation and implementation of ETCI wiring regulations, installation inspection and testing procedures * Utilisation of electrical test instruments * Installation of steel conduit wiring systems * Installation of plastic conduit wiring systems * Installation of steel trunking cable systems * Installation of plastic trunking cable systems * Installation of cable tray and ladder systems * Installation of industrial cable systems * Installation of sub\-distribution boards and protective devices * Installation and maintenance of lighting, heating and motive power systems and controls * Installation and testing of overcurrent and earth leakage protection systems * Installation of earthing and bonding systems * Interpretation of circuit schematics and architectural drawings which incorporate IEC symbols * Interpretation of technical data and manufacturing standards * Interpretation of equipment assembly, disassembly and adjustment procedures * Completion and updating of job\-related documentation; Personal Skills, Common **Skills and Specialist Skills** * System fault analysis skills * Interpretation of electrical/electronic schematic diagrams * Interpretation of flow, function and ladder charts * Soldering and desoldering skills * Testing, removal and replacement of electronic components/PCBs * Installation and programming of PLC systems * Installation and calibration of sensors and transducers * Installation of electro\-pneumatic systems * Installation of data communication cable systems * Analytical ability * Communications * Customer relations * Adaptability * Ability to work as part of a team * Ability to work independently * Initiative and Adaptability * Problem solving * Planning * Information gathering * Quality Assurance * Report Writing * Safety **Minimum Requirements** Leaving Certificate Strong Mathematical ability **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Electrical Engineer (Finland)
description: **Electrical Engineer (Finland)** **Electrical Engineer** required to join one of Europe’s leading Tier 1 Main Contractors on a **Data Center project in Finland.** If you are an **Electrical Engineer** who is looking for your next career move, send your CV to **Oisín O’Donohoe** or call **086 085 7455** for a confidential discussion. **Responsibilities:** * Support preparation of technical and contract documents for distribution * Monitor project progress and produce detailed progress reports * Develop method statements and conduct risk assessments * Liaise with clients, design teams, and contractors on technical issues * Address on\-site technical queries and support electrical engineering tasks, including management, planning, testing, commissioning, and system handover * Plan and manage assigned engineering tasks and projects * Compile bid, approval, and construction drawing packages * Evaluate design effectiveness and process efficiency, proposing improvements as needed * Estimate costs, reliability, and safety considerations * Create specifications for electrical components **Requirements:** * Degree in Electrical Engineering * Minimum 5 years’ experience in a similar role * Experience with Data Centre projects is advantageous * Fluent English with strong communication and interpersonal skills * Proficient in AutoCAD and Microsoft Office * Must hold an EU passport **Benefits:** * Salary €75,000 to €85,000 DOE * Generous living away allowance paid in addition to salary. * Lucrative expat tax scheme * Single occupancy accommodation and utility bills * Regular return flights * In country travel provided * 24 Days annual leave (rising to 30 days after 1 years service) \+ Bank holidays. * Excellent career progression opportunities If you are an **Electrical Engineer** who is looking for your next career move, send your CV to **Oisín O’Donohoe** or call **086 085 7455** for a confidential discussion.

title: Electrical Project Engineer
description: **Electrical Project Engineer** Location(s): Dublin, Cork, Galway, or Kilkenny **Join us in shaping the future of electrical building services and infrastructure delivery.** This is an opportunity for an ambitious Electrical Project Engineer to contribute to high\-quality, technically robust projects across healthcare, public sector, renewable energy, and wider building services programmes, with the support of experienced senior engineers and a collaborative multidisciplinary team. **Purpose \& Scope of the Role** The Electrical Project Engineer will support the delivery of electrical building services projects from concept through to handover, working under the supervision and mentoring of a Senior/Principal Engineer. The role will contribute to feasibility studies, option appraisals, scheme and detailed design, using calculation spreadsheets, specialist design software, AutoCAD, and Revit. You will also visit sites during construction to monitor progress and quality of installation works, while working closely with internal colleagues and wider project teams to help deliver successful outcomes for clients. **Your Impact in This Position** In this role, you will help turn design intent into practical, buildable solutions that meet technical, regulatory, and client requirements. Your work will support the delivery of safe, efficient, and well\-coordinated electrical systems, while contributing to project quality, programme certainty, and strong client satisfaction. You will also play a part in the development of major infrastructure and building projects, helping to shape sustainable long\-term delivery. **Core Capabilities** **Technical Expertise** Strong technical ability across electrical engineering services and design, including MV/LV distribution, lighting, fire alarm, ICT, and life safety systems. Knowledge of Irish electrical design standards and building regulations is essential. **Software \& Digital Design Tools** Experience using AutoCAD, Revit, MS Office, and specialist electrical design software such as Amtech and Dialux to support high\-quality design delivery and documentation. **Communications \& Collaboration** Excellent communication, organisation, and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and work effectively within multidisciplinary project teams. **Project Delivery Support** Ability to contribute to project delivery across all stages, from feasibility and concept design through to construction support and handover, while maintaining quality and attention to detail. **Site \& Construction Awareness** Practical understanding of construction\-stage activities, with the confidence to attend sites, monitor progress, and support the quality of installation works. **Qualifications \& Experience** **Expected** * Minimum Level 8 qualification in Electrical Engineering, or equivalent such as Energy or Building Services. * Minimum 4 years’ experience in electrical building services design. **Beneficial** * Progress toward chartership is strongly preferred. * Equivalent work experience will be considered where appropriate. **Working Environment** This role is based in Dublin, Cork, Galway, or Kilkenny. It operates on a hybrid basis, typically requiring 2–3 days per week in the office, with remote working available where appropriate to project and team needs. Occasional travel to client sites or other offices may be required. Flexible working arrangements are supported where possible, including bank holidays and Fridays, with a focus on sustainable delivery and professional wellbeing. **Why join Tetra Tech?** Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. W e support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are Leading with Science® to solve our clients’ most complex problems. In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role. **Recruitment Agencies** We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list and any submitted will be taken as a gift. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

title: Entreprise Architect, Telco BSS.
description: **In our world things change by the millisecond** We build the technology that creates amazing moments and better connects our customers. Ready to play your part? Join us and Be Phenomenal. Working towards the same shared goal of better connectivity, our tech teams enjoy a high degree of freedom. We love hearing ideas and seeing them come to life. With great resources, you’ll get to be part of a team at the foundations of the transformation of our business. Who knows what the next innovation will be? That’s what makes a career at Three so exciting. **Since we set foot in Ireland, we’ve done things differently** Investing in infrastructure, we built a network that now connects 2\.2 million customers nationwide, carrying more data than all alternative telecommunications providers combined. We take the script and rip it up, we’re a big\-hearted energetic bunch, striving for a better connected life. We encourage our employees to face their weaknesses, to really open themselves up to new tasks and projects in a super\-charged and rich learning environment. We want you to become the best version of yourself. **Join us as a Entreprise Architect, Telco BSS.** The Enterprise Architect determines optimal architectural approaches and solutions to Business requirements across enterprise\-wide applications. They are responsible for the technical correctness and completeness of the end to end system design, including the work of others. They are a key contributor to the planning of the overall IT strategy. This role has a specific focus on the Business Support Systems (BSS) which support the end to end Sales, Ordering, Care \& Billing flows underpinning Three’s products \& services. **As an Enterprise Architect, you will:** * Work closely with business and technical stakeholders to clarify requirements and define the supporting high level architectures. * Govern the progression of architectures, through the various stages of the delivery life cycle, through to implementation * Facilitate and drive the IT decision making process * Support the budgetary process by providing high level ROM estimates for projects * Support the delivery process by providing review and input to project/sprint planning * Create, review and approve solution design artefacts, where appropriate * Be technology agnostic and able to work across all layers of the architecture and across differing technologies and vendors * Contribute to the IT strategy, and be responsible for the evolution of your architecture domain(s) * Keep abreast of new industry trends, patterns, standards or technologies that impact your domain(s) * Maintain the End to End view, where multiple components, suppliers and design artefacts are in play * Work as part of a collaborative IT architecture team, providing support and expertise to your colleagues and leveraging other\`s expertise, when required * Be able to manage multiple and varied suppliers, to collaboratively design and deliver the agreed architecture * Engage with business stakeholders providing support and Technical Leadership to relevant Tribe leaders * Be able to communicate your vision effectively to achieve stakeholder buy\-in **The Skills were looking for:** * Effective collaboration skills, focusing on strong working relationships with all functional units of the organization. * Facilitates discussions and differing perspectives while able to set the course. * Ability to work as part of a cross\-cultural team and multi\-supplier ecosystem, in the office and virtually. * Experience of working in an Agile Methodology * Excellent interpersonal skills in areas such as teamwork, facilitation, and negotiation. * Works well under pressure and is able to manage competing priorities. * Ability to problem solve and recommend creative and innovative technical solutions with standard and non\-standard approaches and technologies. * Additional Qualifications * Knowledge and experience of BSS (Business Support Systems) Architectures in a Telecoms environment * Knowledge and experience of Mobile Virtual Network Enablers (MVNE) solutions * Experience of working with the Amdocs BSS is a plus. * Relevant Degree level education **Benefits of Working at Three** * Flexible working with our new Hybrid model, our employees will enjoy more flexibility working from home and our office locations (3 days per week office based). Please see job description for the office location of this role. * Competitive salary, annual performance bonus \& pension contribution * 25 days holidays plus 2\.5 company days * Annual Leave buy or sell (buy or sell up to 5 days AL each year) * Healthcare Insurance through our flexible benefits programme * Life assurance, phone \& laptop, subsidized canteen * Access to learning \& development tools such as Coursera * Free on\-site parking **There’s a lot more to us than meets the eye.** You may think you know us, but we’re full of surprises. Intrigued? Join us and Be Phenomenal. Apply now at: https://www.three.ie/careers \#Jobs At Three, we are committed to equity, diversity and inclusion. As Ireland’s largest mobile telecommunications provider, we provide a better connected life for our diverse customer base, and want our teams to reflect this. We welcome and celebrate all identities and strive to create a culture of belonging where employees can thrive as their authentic selves. If you do not ‘tick every box’ in this job description, you likely have other valuable skills that would make you a great fit for one of our teams. If you feel this role is for you, then please apply! \#Jobs If you require reasonable adjustments for your interview, please let us know when scheduling your interview, or alternatively, please email aoife.omeara@three.ie **Apply now at Three, a Phenomenal career awaits!** awaits!

title: FX Dealer
description: Who We Are: Moneycorp powers global payments for corporates, institutions, and high net worth individuals, combining worldwide reach with local expertise. With over 45 years of market experience, two banking licences and offices in eleven countries, our technology delivers 24/7 accessibility, while our people deliver award\-winning service. In 2024, we supported $1tn\+ of payment volume, reaching 190 countries in 130 currencies. Our 32k\+ corporate clients benefit from our regulatory resilience with 63\+ permissions globally, access to major payment rails, and our proprietary technology. Moneycorp is a place where energy, commitment, and collaboration are core to our values of shared success. We are restless in our drive to surpass the expectations of our customers and unlock opportunities to support them at every stage of their journey. The foundation of our success is our people, and nurturing a culture of belonging for all our colleagues is central to our journey as a global business. Role Purpose: We are looking for a commercially driven FX Dealer to join our Dublin team. This is a client\-facing role which combines FX dealing and relationship management, with responsibility for managing and growing a portfolio of corporate and SME clients. Experience in FX, international payments, treasury sales, or financial services is required. You will deliver tailored FX and international payments solutions, execute transactions, and proactively identify opportunities to grow revenue and deepen client relationships. This role offers strong development opportunities within a growing European business, with the potential to progress into senior dealing or leadership positions. Role Responsibilities: Client Relationship Management * Deliver high\-quality client service across trade execution, payments, and queries, ensuring a responsive and professional experience * Monitor FX markets and provide relevant insights and commentary to clients Commercial Performance * Work towards individual and team revenue targets, activity metrics, and portfolio growth objectives * Use market knowledge and client insight to spot opportunities and support business growth * Maintain a strong focus on conversion, client activity, and relationship development * Contribute ideas and best practice to help improve overall desk performance * Proactively identify and develop new business opportunities within existing and prospective clients * Confidently quote FX rates and manage pricing within agreed risk parameters Risk, Compliance \& Controls * Maintain accurate and timely records across dealing systems and D365 * Ensure that all client interactions, follow\-ups, and dealing activity is recorded appropriately * Adhere to all company policies and procedures, with particular attention to KYC, AML, and regulatory requirements * Escalate client complaints and suspicious activity in line with company guidelines What We Are Looking For: Essential Knowledge, Skills \& Experience: * 2–5 years’ experience in FX dealing, FX sales, international payments, treasury sales, or a related financial services environment * Strong understanding of FX products including spot, forwards, and hedging strategies * Good awareness of macroeconomic drivers and their impact on FX markets * Experience executing FX transactions accurately and efficiently while providing market insight to clients * Confidence quoting and managing pricing within defined risk parameters * Experience managing and growing client relationships in a commercial, target\-driven environment * Proven ability to generate leads, convert opportunities, and build a portfolio of clients * Strong understanding of corporate or SME client needs in relation to FX and/or international payments * Confidence engaging and influencing stakeholders at varying levels of seniority * Experience working across multiple channels, including telephone, email, and client meetings * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively * Clear and professional communication skills * Good working knowledge of CRM systems, Excel, and Word * Strong commercial awareness, negotiation skills, and client\-first mindset * Ability to work closely with Credit, Compliance and Payments teams to deliver a seamless client experience Desirable Knowledge, Skills \& Experience: * Experience in a non\-bank FX provider or fintech environment * Knowledge of D365 or similar CRM platforms * Experience covering SME and mid\-market corporates Education / Qualifications: * A third‑level qualification in business, finance, economics, or a related discipline is preferred Languages: * English (fluent) What We Can Offer You: This role offers a competitive salary (commensurate with experience), participation in discretionary bonus scheme, 25 days annual leave, pension and health contributions, life assurance, and bike to work scheme. Location and Contract Type: This is a full\-time, permanent position. This role is office\-based in our Dublin dealing room. Fostering a culture of belonging and inclusivity We are committed to creating a workplace where every individual feels valued, respected, and included. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we actively cultivate an inclusive culture where diversity thrives, and we empower our colleagues to drive meaningful change within our organization through Employee Engagement initiatives and value champion networks. We recognise that fostering inclusivity is an ongoing journey, and we remain steadfast in our commitment to progress. By measuring our efforts through regular assessments and listening to the feedback of our employees, we strive to ensure that our initiatives are impactful and responsive to the evolving needs of our workforce. Together, we want to build a workplace where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work, as we believe this is the foundation of innovation, creativity, and collective success. Interested? If this role sounds like a good fit for you, please click on the “Apply Now” button to submit your application. To discover more about Moneycorp and how we’re leading the way in global payments and currency risk management, please visit our News Hub and various social media platforms Facebook, Twitter UK, Twitter Americas, Instagram, LinkedIn.

title: Facilities Assistant
description: **Job Description** **Exceptional Hospitality Starts with You** Picture yourself brightening someone’s day. When you join our Hotels team, that’s exactly what you’ll do as a Facilities Assistant, you’re not just helping maintain the hotel’s physical safety and functionality – you’re spreading the light and warmth of hospitality by delivering memorable experiences that make the stay for every guest. **Responsibilities** Here's what you'll do during a typical day: * Support hotel facility maintenance Assist in maintaining the physical functionality and safety of the property, including guest rooms, public spaces, and heart\-of\-house areas: Support hotel facility maintenance Assist in maintaining the physical functionality and safety of the property, including guest rooms, public spaces, and heart\-of\-house areas * Respond to service requests: Quickly handle guest calls and team member work orders * Support a variety of maintenance tasks: Conduct repairs and maintenance across various areas, including carpentry, plumbing, electrical, HVAC and masonry * Assist with preventive maintenance inspections: Inspect facilities and equipment to identify and address preventive maintenance needs * Document maintenance activities: Record completed inspections and repairs and flag any issues requiring further attention **Qualifications** **What It Takes to Make the Stay** At Hilton, Our Core Values Define What It Takes To Succeed Here And Guide The Qualities We Look For In Every Team Member A passion for spreading the light and warmth of **Hospitality** . Acting with **Integrity** and always doing the right thing. Inspiring others through **Leadership** . A belief that **Teamwork** drives the best outcomes. A sense of **Ownership** and accountability. And a focus on the **Now** , bringing urgency and discipline to every moment, knowing it can make a lasting impact. **About Us** **Join an Award\-Winning Workplace Culture** At Hilton, we don’t just deliver exceptional experiences for our guests—we build an exceptional workplace for the Team Members who make it all possible. As a global leader in hospitality, we’ve welcomed more than 3 billion guests worldwide, all while staying true to our founding vision: to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality. Our award\-winning culture has earned us repeated recognition on the World’s Best Workplaces list by Great Place to Work and Fortune. With our suite of world\-class brands , and a company\-wide commitment to providing the best stay for every guest, we’re setting new standards for the future of travel. Whether you’re starting your career or exploring something new, Hilton supports your journey every step of the way. Come for the job, stay for the career—and help us make every stay a little more magical. Curious about life at Hilton? Explore our Careers Blog to see why we’re more than a great place to stay—we’re a great place to work. **About The Team** **How We’ll Help You Thrive** **Benefits** At Hilton, the hospitality we’re known for doesn’t end with our guests. We proudly invest in our Team Members’ wellbeing, supporting you through all of life’s moments. When you join Hilton, our exceptional care extends to you with unmatched perks and benefits, including\*: * Incredible travel perks – Enjoy 110 nights of deeply discounted travel, with room rates as low as $40 USD/night at our world\-class hotels through our Go Hilton travel program * Own a piece of Hilton – Through our employee stock purchase program (ESPP), you can invest in Hilton shares at a 15% discount to help build your financial future * Paid parental leave – Because family matters. We offer paid leave for eligible Team Members, including partners and adoptive parents * Personalized caregiving support – Access dedicated caregiving concierge services to help navigate caregiving for yourself and your loved ones * Crisis concierge – In times of loss, our Crisis Concierge offers a single, compassionate point of contact for both practical support and emotional care * Mental health resources – Your wellbeing comes first. Through our Care for All hub, we provide resources to help our Team Members to care for themselves, care for members of their team, and care for their loved ones. Eligible Team Members receive free counseling and support through our best\-in\-class Employee Assistance Program (EAP) * Benefits availability may vary depending on Team Member’s location as well as terms and conditions of employment and are subject to the terms and conditions of each specific program/plan.

title: Fashion Brand & Sales Development Assistant
description: **Fashion Brand \& Sales Development Assistant** ​**Company**: Silk \& Sequins **Location**: Carlow Town, Co. Carlow (Remote\-First / Hybrid) **Job Type:** Part\-Time, Fixed\-Term (4\-Week Initial Trial ) **Hours**: 5 hours per week (Highly flexible scheduling) **Salary**: €14\.15 per hour (\+ 8% holiday pay) \+ 10% commission on all successful retail accounts secured. ​**About Silk \& Sequins** ​Silk \& Sequins is an established fashion house transitioning its signature heritage of bespoke, high\-end tailoring into an exclusive, ultra\-luxury **Ready to Wear** line. Driven by high\-octane glamour, structural drama, and unapologetic femininity, our collections focus on premium garment architecture and show\-stopping evening wear. ​As we scale, we are launching a targeted wholesale strategy to place our pieces into premium, independent luxury retail boutiques across Ireland and Europe. ***We are seeking a sharp, stylish, and self\-motivated assistant to act as the commercial engine behind this launch.*** ​**The Role \& Future Opportunity** ​This is a **micro\-part\-time, remote\-first role** (5 hours per week) designed for an ambitious fashion/marketing student, recent graduate, or freelance professional who wants to build an incredible portfolio. ​**We are explicitly looking for a long\-term partner to grow with the brand.** To ensure a perfect mutual fit, the role will begin with an initial 4\-week trial period. Upon successful completion, this position is intended to transition into a permanent, long\-term role as our retail network expands. ​You will work directly with the Brand Founder to manage B2B outreach and elevate our brand presence. This role is a perfect blend of digital research, creative luxury marketing, and real\-world commercial pitches. ​**Key Responsibilities:** ​**Wholesale Market Research:** Identify, vet, and map out high\-end independent boutiques across Ireland that cater to premium occasion wear and luxury evening wear clientele. ​**B2B Sales Outreach:** Build and manage structured Excel/Google Sheet lead trackers, drafting and executing sophisticated pitch emails to retail buyers. ​**In\-Person Boutique Pitching:** Visit target luxury boutiques—either alongside the Brand Founder or independently—to present the collection, display the garment architecture, and secure wholesale accounts. ​**Brand \& Content Alignment:** Collaborate on creative ideas for our social media channels to ensure our digital presence looks pristine, high\-end, and highly attractive to boutique owners. ​**Studio Alignment (Occasional)**: Avail of flexible, periodic visits to our Carlow studio to review new garment architecture, understand the craftsmanship, and capture authentic behind\-the\-scenes content. ​**Requirements**: ​A profound understanding of, and passion for, the luxury fashion industry (evening wear, premium tailoring, and high\-end design). ​Confident, professional communication skills with the ability to pitch to retail buyers face\-to\-face and via email. ​High organizational discipline—you must be comfortable managing spreadsheets and tracking leads accurately. ​Familiarity with digital content tools (e.g., Canva, CapCut, social media editing) is a strong advantage. ​Based in or within reasonable commuting distance of Co. Carlow for occasional studio alignment and regional boutique visits. ​**Benefits**:A direct 10% commission on the wholesale value of all successful accounts you secure. ​**Long\-Term Career Growth:** The opportunity to establish yourself early within a growing luxury brand with a view to long\-term employment. ​**Ultimate Flexibility:** Choose your own working hours weekly to easily fit around your college classes or other freelance projects. ​**Direct Mentorship:** Work one\-on\-one with the fashion designer and founder, gaining rare, face\-to\-face experience in luxury retail placement. ​**To Apply:** ​Please submit your CV along with a brief note detailing your passion for the luxury fashion industry and why you are drawn to the world of high\-glamour evening wear. ​Applications close within 7 days. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a brief 15\-minute introductory video call. Job Types: Part\-time, Fixed term Pay: €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Employee discount * Flexitime * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Carlow, County Carlow

title: Float Secretary
description: ### **Description** DAC Beachcroft Dublin is looking to recruit a Float Legal Secretary to work within the office assisting teams in general. We are seeking a secretary who is accustomed to working in a busy environment. The role will be office based with working hours of Monday to Friday 10am to 6pm and full training will be given. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Preparing correspondence and documents from digital dictation * Preparing briefs * Creating, amending and formatting legal documents and templates with a high degree of accuracy * Updating the MI databases * File management, including/both physical and electronic filing, scanning, photocopying and archiving * Liaising with experts/counsel * Arranging meetings/consultations * Diary management * Assist with expense claims * Booking of meeting rooms * Issuing large documents such as briefs to Counsel/experts via OneDrive * Billing preparation and issuing bills to clients * Debt collection * Booking travel ### **Skills, Knowledge \& Expertise** * Proven experience of working as a Legal Secretary however full training will be given * Strong dictation experience * A minimum of 60 wpm * Proficient use of Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, PDF Docs and OneDrive * Strong attention to detail and accuracy is essential * Excellent administration skills * Ability to work on one's own initiative * Ability to draft correspondence when asked to do so * Excellent organisational skills * Ability to prioritise large workloads * Ability to multi\-task and work to strict deadlines * Team orientated and collaborative with a flexible attitude * Professional telephone manner and excellent communication skills, both written and oral * The candidate should have a positive, helpful and cheerful attitude * Experience of preparing briefs ### **About DAC Beachcroft** We are a leading international legal business with over 3,000 colleagues and a diverse range of capabilities. At DAC Beachcroft we are committed to and recognise the importance of equity, diversity and inclusion across the board, including and beyond the protected characteristics. We take decisions based solely on merit. We value, welcome and seek out people with different backgrounds and life experiences, perspectives, beliefs, ways of thinking and identities. We are happy to talk flexible working with our Flex Forward scheme. When external support is required, roles are released to selected agencies on our Preferred Supplier List (PSL). Speculative CVs sent to any DAC Beachcroft employee without prior instruction from our recruitment teams (LLP and CSG) will not be accepted, and no fees will be payable. For PSL queries, please contact: recruitment@dacbeachcroft.com or csgrecruitment@dacbeachcroft.com

title: Founding Engineer
description: Roughly a third of all the food the world produces is thrown away, and after households, the kitchens that feed us in hotels, offices, hospitals and universities are among the biggest wasters of all. A single commercial kitchen can bin tens of thousands of euro of food a year without ever seeing it on a balance sheet. It is one of the largest, most solvable problems in climate today, and almost nobody can measure it. Positive Carbon makes it measurable. Founded in Dublin in 2020, we build AI that sees what a kitchen throws away. Our sensor sits above any bin and uses computer vision, the same technology found in self\-driving cars, to automatically capture the weight, volume and type of every item discarded, with no manual logging by busy kitchen staff. That turns an invisible operational habit into clear, real\-time financial data: what is being wasted, what it costs, and where to stop it. The results are real. Kitchens using Positive Carbon have cut the value of the food they waste by up to 90%, lifting gross profit by 2–8% and returning several times what they spend with us. We work with hospitality groups, universities and workplace caterers across Ireland, the UK and the DACH region And we are still small. That is the opportunity. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. We're hiring our first in\-house engineer. Until now our platform has been built with an external development partner; you'll bring it in\-house, own it, and set the technical direction from here. This is a founding role in the real sense your decisions on architecture, tooling and engineering culture will stick. Tasks * Own the Positive Carbon platform end to end: the food\-waste capture pipeline, the data and analytics layer, and the dashboards customers use to find and reduce wasted\-food cost. * Turn raw measurement into financial insight waste value by site, category and meal period. * Build AI into the product as a first\-class capability, not a bolt\-on: agents and LLM pipelines for enrichment, reporting, anomaly detection and operational recommendations. * Use AI aggressively in how you build. We expect a small team to ship like a much larger one. * Stand up the engineering foundations we'll need to scale: CI/CD, observability, data quality and a sane release process. * Transition us cleanly off the agency setup without disrupting live customers. Requirements * Startup or founding experience (must\-have): you've worked at an early\-stage company or in a founding/first\-hire role and you're at home with ambiguity, ownership and a blank page. * Strong full\-stack experience across backend, frontend and data and the instinct to go deep wherever the hard problem is. * A track record of shipping production software that real customers rely on. * Genuine fluency with modern AI tooling you build with LLMs and agents and have opinions on where they help and where they don't. * Pragmatism over perfection: you scope hard, ship, and iterate. * Comfort being the technical owner when there's no one else to ask. Benefits * Health Insurance * Pension * Bike to work * 25 Days Annual Leave * Hybrid Work * Computer vision, IoT/edge or data\-pipeline experience. * Experience as a founding engineer or very early hire. * Interest in the climate and food\-systems problem. **Send a short note on why this role, plus a CV or LinkedIn, or through our website. Tell us about something you built, shipped or closed not just where you worked.**

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: LotusWorks is an Engineering Services provider specializing in managing Commissioning, Construction Services, Calibration, Operations \& Maintenance scopes of work within global manufacturing facilities. LotusWorks operates globally across EMEA and North America partnering with the world's leading manufacturers across the Semiconductor, Pharmaceutical \& Biologics, Medical Device and Data Centre sectors. Our team of Engineering and Technical professionals is immersed in cutting\-edge technologies and innovations. LotusWorks is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, integral to our people\-first approach. As a Graduate Electrical Engineer (Commissioning), you will support the Electrical Commissioning Team in the execution of electrical commissioning activities on Data Centre projects across Europe. This role combines hands\-on field commissioning with office\-based engineering support, providing an excellent opportunity to develop technical expertise in electrical systems, infrastructure, controls, and commissioning processes. Working alongside experienced engineers and technicians, you will receive structured training, mentoring, and field exposure to build competence in commissioning activities from Level 2 (Installation Verification) through to Level 5 (Integrated System Testing). This role is designed to support your progression from a graduate engineer to an independent commissioning professional. Travel to project sites across Europe for extended periods is a core requirement of this position. **Responsibilities** * Support electrical commissioning activities across Data Centre projects under the guidance of experienced commissioning personnel. * Participate in electrical design reviews and assist in identifying and documenting design discrepancies. * Review project specifications, control philosophies, System of Operation (SOO), Description of Operation (DOO), and equipment submittals. * Read and interpret electrical drawings, including single\-line diagrams, schematics, and layouts. * Assist in the development and review of commissioning test scripts and procedures. * Witness and support Level 2 to Level 5 commissioning activities, including Installation Verification Checks (IVC), Pre\-Functional Checks (PFC), Functional Performance Tests (FPT), and Integrated System Tests (IST). * Execute assigned commissioning activities once trained and authorised by the Electrical Lead. * Accurately record test results, field observations, and commissioning documentation. * Assist with preparation of commissioning reports, trackers, dashboards, and As\-Left documentation. * Attend project meetings and provide status updates on assigned activities. * Support the review of commissioning documentation and document management systems. * Any other duties or responsibilities that may be assigned to you in your role. **Skills Required** * Strong interest in electrical engineering and commissioning within mission\-critical environments. * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, schematics, and engineering documentation. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational abilities. * Effective written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. * Willingness to learn and develop technical knowledge through training and practical experience. * Ability to travel extensively across Europe for project assignments. * Commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality. **Education / Licenses / Certifications** * Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related engineering discipline. * Recent graduates or candidates with relevant internship/work placement experience are encouraged to apply. * Full driving licence preferred. * Proof of education, licenses, and certifications will be required where applicable **Benefits** * VHI Healthcare Cover * Life Assurance * Pension Contribution * Training And Educational Assistance * Annual GP Check\-Up * Extra Annual Leave * Healthcare Screening * Recognition Rewards *LotusWorks is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees feel valued, respected, and empowered.*

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Greek Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and Greek * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: IRE_Quality Systems Specialist
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Bridge to thousands of biopharma companies and their patients. **We are PCI.** Our investment is in People who make an impact, drive progress and create a better tomorrow. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. **Job Title:** Quality Systems Specialist JD061 (level 1\) **Department:** Quality **Division / Section:** Quality Assurance **Accountable to:** Quality Systems Supervisor **Summary of Role** The role is to implement and execute tasks relating to day\-to\-day Quality Assurance aspects. The role entails taking responsibility for Quality Assurance matters around processes and documentation in the City North business unit. **Key Responsibilities** * Ensure safe work practices are being followed at all times, report any near misses immediately to the EHS Department. * Writing and reviewing SOP's, Work Instructions, BPR's and Forms. * Writing and reviewing of Deviations, Non\-Conformance reports, Corrective Action Requests and prepare the final report for QP review. * Follow up on change control actions with appropriate action owners and drive for on time closure of actions. * Prepare Quarterly reports for each site. * Prepare Product Quality Review reports. * Prepare \& present Monthly/Annual Metrics. * Complete complaints investigations and assist with close out of complaints. * Perform cGMP internal audits. * Assist in the performance of external audits (as required). * Responsible for executing training for quality \& other relevant persons. * Assist with preparation for customer/regulatory audits. * Assist with audit requests during customer/regulatory audits. * Prepare responses to customer audits. * Host/attend cross departmental meetings as required as quality representation. * Identify and support opportunities for improving processes and or procedures. * To undertake such tasks and to manage specific or ad hoc projects as and when required to meet department and business needs and within the scope of the job\-holder’s capabilities. * Carry out various other additional tasks that may be required from time to time in accordance with the overall goal of the company **Knowledge / Skills \& Experience** **Essential:** * Bachelor’s or associate degree or Diploma / Certificate in GMP, Regulatory and Compliance or similar. May substitute experience in lieu of educational requirements. * 1\+ years’ experience working in GMP or Regulated Environment * Ability to manage multiple tasks and set priorities. * Problem Solving and Troubleshooting skills. Excel, PowerPoint, Word, etc.) * Driving License Required. **Desirable:** * Experience in Lean Manufacturing, including experience in Lean tools to develop continuous improvements. title: In-House Content Creator / Videographer
description: **Company Overview** **The** **Wow\-Inspiring Group** leads the way in the creation of eye\-catching and **Wow\-Inspiring** walls, signs and displays in the places where we live, work and play, by expertly supplying the materials, equipment, tools and know\-how to those who design and build them. description: Our Dublin based client are looking for an **Information Technology Support Analyst** to join their team on a permanent basis You will have the opportunity to become part of an established and strong team and work for a company that is a market leader in their field There is an on site presence required for this role and the office is Dublin city centre based **Responsibilities:** * Provide technical support to end\-users and assist in resolving various IT issues in an effective and timely manner. * Learn to identify and resolve IT problems and issues as they arise * Troubleshoot hardware and software issues on site and via cloud services * Assist in the setup, configuration, and maintenance of end\-user devices, laptops and phones * Maintain a highly organised inventory of IT assets and resources (ITAM). * Assist in monitoring and tracking issues to prevent problems occurring a second or third time. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure escalations and tickets are registered and dealt with effectively. * Contribute to IT projects, including software deployments and hardware upgrades. * Escalation of technical issues to other team members when necessary and required **Skills and Requirements:** * Minimum of 2\-3 years of experience in a technical support role. * Experience with Active Directory, Azure AD, and managing security groups. * Ability to handle access requests and managing permissions. * Proven capabilities in troubleshooting Office 365 issues and software installations. * Proactive problem\-solving skills. * Have strong verbal and written communication skills. * Demonstrated teamwork skills, attention to detail, and a strong work ethic. * Enthusiasm for technology and a desire to learn and grow in the field. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. title: Infrastructure Engineer
description: Our client is seeking an experienced Infrastructure Engineer to join their IT Service Operations team. This hands\-on role is responsible for supporting and enhancing core infrastructure, Microsoft 365 services, network operations, and security platforms. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high\-performing IT services while contributing to infrastructure projects and continuous improvement initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Administer and support Microsoft 365, including Exchange Online, Entra ID, and Intune. * Manage Windows and Linux servers, virtualisation platforms, and cloud infrastructure. * Support networking and firewall technologies, including Cisco and Palo Alto solutions. * Troubleshoot complex infrastructure, network, and Microsoft 365 issues. * Deliver infrastructure projects from planning through implementation. * Coordinate with external service providers and support partners to ensure effective service delivery. * Maintain security controls through monitoring, vulnerability management, and patching. * Conduct Disaster Recovery (DR) testing and maintain technical documentation. * Contribute to ongoing service improvements and operational excellence. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Third\-level qualification in Information Technology or a related discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience in infrastructure engineering, systems engineering, or IT operations. * Strong Microsoft 365 administration and systems engineering experience. * Good understanding of networking, firewall technologies, cloud platforms, and security best practices. * Experience working within a regulated environment would be advantageous. **Desirable Certifications** * Azure Administrator Associate (AZ\-104\) * Intune Administrator (MD\-102\) * Cisco CCNA * Palo Alto PCNSA * CompTIA Security\+ * ITIL 4 Foundation **Key Competencies** * Customer Relationship Management * Planning and Organisation * Problem Solving and Decision Making * Strong Communication and Interpersonal Skills * Collaboration and Teamwork * Drive for Results **How to Apply** To be considered for this opportunity, please submit your latest CV.

title: Inside Sales Representative (Inbound) - Mailchimp
description: **About MarketStar:** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. We are excited to have you apply to join our MarketStar team and can’t wait to discuss how we can help you find growth! **About The Inbound Inside Sales Representative:** MarketStar is hiring an Inside Sales Representative to represent our Mailchimp client, a world\-class business marketing tool. If you are passionate about cutting edge and emerging technology and are ready to join a successful and fast growing sales program at MarketStar, we have a great opportunity for you. This is an opportunity to be part of a super interesting journey, at the forefront of the latest emerging technology as Mailchimp continues its growth into the EMEA market. Your responsibility will be to create and close a pipeline of inbound leads that can contribute to the company's growth and success. You will deal primarily with prospects across the EMEA region, although the target audience and region could be subject to change and expansion as the team evolves. You will be required to manage a inbound leads, win business, and hit set KPIs on a monthly and quarterly basis. As you grow and excel in your role, there will be ample opportunities for career advancement within our organization. We're invested in your long\-term success and are excited to see you thrive with us. **Location:** Hybrid, Leopardstown, Dublin 18 (Tuesday \& Thursday in office) **What Will You Do?** * Execute versus a monthly/quarterly target in the small to medium enterprise segment * Manage a flow of inbound leads across a number of channels, driving pipeline and delivering against a set of KPIs to close new business * Demonstrate a strong knowledge of the Mailchimp product suite, with the capacity to minimize the sales cycle through a chosen sales methodology * Serve as product and service advocate for the client building interest and awareness of client’s value proposition * Effectively manage and prioritize a pipeline of sales opportunities, tracking their progress and ensuring timely follow\-ups * Execute effective sales strategies to maximize the conversion of leads into paying customers * Provide effective account management to support customer’s technology strategy, implementation, and future requirements * Collaborate with the leadership team and our client to identify strategies, and processes to better achieve revenue goals **What Will You Need to Succeed?** * Native level of English * 18 – 24 months SDR in SaaS/ IT Services space mid\-market to enterprise sales or 6 – 12 months in B2B sales as individual contributor \- desired * Experience with software vendor, SaaS space or system integrator space is a huge plus * Exceptional skills in prospecting, discovery and presentation * Strong sales aptitude skills, solutions oriented with a passion for the customer and the customer experience * Highly collaborative and results\-oriented with proven success in sales attainment and ability to excel in a team environment * Tech\-savvy with an affinity for innovative and emerging technology * Self\-reliant, adaptable, decisive, and professional; able to effectively multi\-task in a dynamic environment * Expert ability to serve as product and service advocate for the client building interest and awareness of client’s value proposition * Ability to quickly articulate technical and business value propositions via phone and email * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, including outstanding sales presence and presentation skills **What We Offer:** In our commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Education reimbursement * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards **Compensation Range:** €42,000 \- €50,000 EUR *The pay for this position is basic salary per annum of €42,000* *with an additional variable bonus component of €8,000* . *There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process.* *MarketStar is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual with a disability and require reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at* people.success@marketstar.com *for assistance.*

title: Integration Engineer (Client facing) Professional Services
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Stripe’s Professional Services team works with our most strategic users. You will work on the highest visibility projects to provide technical expertise to our customers to help accelerate their knowledge, solution design and integrations with Stripe. This role is responsible for leading strategic conversations and developing plans that will transform the payments and financial infrastructure for leading enterprises. What you’ll do Responsibilities \* Plan and execute integration engineering end\-to\-end, from high\-level architecture down to code implementation. This includes conducting discovery sessions with users to understand their goals, parsing out specific use cases, and guiding users to implement high complexity integrations. * Think on your feet and communicate technical concepts, explaining how Stripe works from both a product feature and API architecture perspective. Manage user expectations on timelines and product changes. * Be the primary, ongoing technical point of contact for our largest users' and partners' technical teams. You will be responsible for resolving user integration issues and owning escalations for operational issues and priority requests. * Collaborate with Sales, Solution Architecture and Implementation teams to drive client engagement, customer wins, and revenue on behalf of the Professional Services team * Collaborate closely with Product and Engineering to proactively identify potential enhancements and new features based on feedback you’ll gather from the field. * Provide deep technical support and debugging, inspecting and understanding the Stripe codebase and thoroughly testing functionality to best advise users, while also building and demonstrating proof\-of\-concept integrations, creating user\-specific test plans, and answering ad\-hoc technical questions. * In some cases, lead "Hands On Keyboard" implementations for user engagements, providing material feedback on coding practices, CI/CD, and software architecture. * Review and optimize existing user integrations and provide recommendations to improve performance, reliability, and efficiency. * Build tools and analytics; create dashboards, run queries, and generate custom data reports to support user business reviews and operational needs. * Develop and share knowledge, building reusable processes to enable the larger Professional Services team. Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements \* 4 years \+ experience in a Software Engineering / Integration Engineering role. * Ability to write code (in programming languages like Go, Java, C/C\+\+ etc.) and quickly debug user code snippets. * Comfortable with various code\-level debugging (Stripe code and user code) and using code as a tool for problem\-solving. * Strong generalist technical experience, including proficiency in SQL and an understanding of data structures and algorithms. * Understanding of the payments domain and API security topics. * Be comfortable talking about technical concepts, and explaining how Stripe works, both from a product features and API architecture perspective. * A user first approach \& empathy with our users, capable of mapping business requirements into reliable technical solutions. * Confident and comfortable with customers, with the ability to lead strategic technical conversations. Preferred requirements \* Enjoy the puzzle of solving open\-ended problems, both individually and as a member of our team. Willingness to learn and teach others, including mentoring newer team members. * Collaborative, communicative, consultative; able to apply specific methodologies to ensure repeatability of engagements. * Intellectually curious, with great problem solving skills. * Technical writing skills to document complex integration guidance and processes. In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €104,000 \- €156,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Junior JavaScript Developer
description: Arabian Private Holdings is looking for a Junior JavaScript Developer to contribute to the development of our internal proprietary tools. This role is focused on building functional interfaces that translate complex corporate data into readable formats for our firm principals. We are currently collaborating with a research and software group in Ireland to build out a new data\-visualization layer for our portfolio holding companies. This specific activity requires technical support that can coordinate with these partners during their business hours while working as part of our remote distributed team. **Employment Setup** This is a fully remote role where the legal employer is Arabian Private Holdings, based in Saudi Arabia. This position is open to candidates based in Ireland who already possess the legal right to work in their country of residence, as we do not provide visa sponsorship or local employment contracts. The arrangement is a full\-time, permanent engagement with attractive pay provided. You will report directly to our Lead Technical Architect. Our hiring process involves an initial review of your technical background, a small JavaScript coding exercise, and a final conversation with a firm principal. We require at least four hours of overlap with the GMT\+3 timezone to facilitate team coordination and peer reviews. **About Arabian Private Holdings** Our firm was founded on the principle that many critical business problems do not require a massive consulting team or a hundred\-page deck. Instead, they require a direct and thorough view of the situation. We advise family groups, listed corporations, and government entities on corporate strategy and operating model performance. Because we focus on simplicity over complexity, our digital tools must be equally direct. We operate as a remote firm, choosing to prioritize the quality of our analysis and the strength of our relationships over traditional office space. This structure allows us to focus entirely on the mandates we accept, ensuring that every piece of software we build serves a specific, logical purpose for our clients. **What You Will Do** * Develop and maintain UI components using modern JavaScript to support our portfolio dashboards. * Translate logic from strategy models into interactive web\-based visualizations. * Work closely with the Lead Architect to maintain a clean and lightweight codebase. * Debug frontend performance issues to ensure our reporting tools remain responsive. * Write straightforward documentation for the features and components you build. * Collaborate with the strategy team to refine how complex ownership data is displayed. * Implement responsive designs that allow principals to access data on various devices. * Participate in peer code reviews to ensure consistency across our internal tools and systems. **What We Are Looking For** * Basic familiarity with JavaScript (ES6\+) and an understanding of core programming logic. * Some exposure to modern frontend libraries such as React, Vue, or Svelte. * Ability to write clean HTML and CSS to create functional, distraction\-free interfaces. * A basic understanding of version control systems like Git for code management. * Attention to detail when following design specifications or data requirements. * Comfort working in a remote environment that relies heavily on written communication. * An interest in learning how technical tools can support high\-level corporate decision\-making. **What This Role Offers** * Direct mentorship from a Lead Technical Architect with deep industry knowledge. * The opportunity to build professional tools used by senior leaders in major organizations. * A working environment that values deep focus and logical precision over meetings. * A fully remote setup that allows for a stable and consistent professional routine. * Exposure to the intersection of frontend development and high\-level corporate strategy. * Professional growth within a firm that prioritizes directness and technical quality.

title: Junior Marketing Executive
description: We're looking for a curious, driven professional who's passionate about marketing and wants to grow its strategic experience. If you're someone who spots a great shot, has a strong eye for design, and is curious to learn and ambitious to grow we are looking for you. This isn't a desk\-only role. You'll be out on\-site, capturing the work we do, and then turning that into compelling content across our channels. The role sits at the heart of the business, reporting directly to the Sales Manager and working closely with, fabrication, purchasing, and external partners. **Who We Are** Celuplast is a fast\-growing specialist supplier and fabricator of building products, serving trade markets across Ireland. We operate across trade counter, online sales, fabrication systems, and specialist product categories including landscaping, interiors, roofing, and home extension solutions. We're part of a wider group with four companies across Ireland and the UK, all focused on building products. That means there's a broader network to collaborate with, share ideas across, and for the right person, plenty of room to grow. **What You'll Be Doing** **Day\-to\-Day** • Create and manage content across our social media channels, keeping things consistent, on\-brand, and engaging • Get out on\-site to photograph and film our products, projects, and people — strong photography skills are a must • Updating the Marketing Calendar • Edit video content for use across social, web, and other channels • Coordinate with website developer so the message is consistent across product listings and landing pages. • Plan and send email marketing campaigns (at least twice a month) to trade and homeowner audiences • Support product launches with well\-timed, well\-designed assets across all channels • Collaborate with our PIM system to keep product information accurate and up to date • Create marketing collateral including brochures, flyers, presentations and digital assets. • Learn and contribute to campaign planning and execution. **What We're Looking For** **Essential** • 3\+ years of experience in design and content creation. • Strong photography skills — comfortable shooting on\-site in a real\-world, non\-studio environment • Video editing skills • Social media management experience • Experience with email marketing tools and campaigns • A genuine eye for design this is a big asset in how we evaluate candidates • Proactivity and curiosity. • Excellent Communication Skills **Nice to Have** • Google Analytics Experience • Experience working with KPIs and reporting on campaign performance • Familiarity with PIM systems or product data management • Some exposure to paid media or basic campaign strategy • Experience in a trade, construction, or building products environment • Experience in e\-commerce. **What Success Looks Like** We're not just looking for activity, we want progress. Here's how we'll measure it together: • We’ll create a plan to grow our social media following (targeting new followers per month) • Maintaining a steady organic engagement across channels • Generating leads through social media and website activity • Running email campaigns twice a month, on time and on brief • Delivering monthly reports that show ROI and conversion metrics, and knowing what to do with the data **Why Join Us?** You'll be joining a business that's growing, has a clear direction, and genuinely wants its marketing to match its ambitions. This is a role where you can shape things, not just maintain them, and build a track record that could take you into a senior position within the group. Beyond the work itself, we're a team that looks after each other. You'll find good craic in the day\-to\-day, regular social events, and a culture that takes giving back seriously as we actively support non\-profit causes as part of who we are. You'll also get real exposure to the wider building and construction community across Ireland and the UK, expanding your professional network in an industry that rewards relationships. If you're ready to get your hands dirty, pick up a camera, and make a real difference to how a growing Irish business presents itself to the world, we'd love to hear from you.

At Cairn, it’s not what we build, it’s why we build. It’s about putting down a marker that will stand for generations to come. Creating new communities of connection and belonging for an Ireland where people can thrive. Reshaping, redefining, reinvigorating our place in the world. Building for people, progress, and potential. Because when Cairn build, it’s Built For Good.
