title: Junior Systems Analyst
description: Canada Life Europe (CLE) employs over 600 people across its Irish and German offices and serves over 500,000 customers in the German market. It focuses on pensions and individual protection products, offering a combination of unit\-linked funds, unitised\-with\-profits funds and variable annuity business within its pension products. Currently CLE is amongst the top six companies in the German broker market and its goal is to become a Top 3 Broker Provider in the market. CLE is seeking a Junior Systems Analyst to join our System Analysts’ team within Information Services (I.S.). The overall I.S. function has over 120 people in the team. **Role Overview** Canada Life Europe requires a Junior Systems Analyst to work on I.S. projects, initially on the Point of Sales Delivery team. This individual will work with the I.S. and Business teams based in Germany and Ireland, and the Canada Life Partners and Brokers based in Germany. The successful candidate will be assigned to work under the direction of the Lead Analyst. As a team member he/she will be expected to work effectively with other team members such as Systems and QA Analysts, Project Managers and Software Developers. This role is part of the I.S. Systems Analysis Team which is predominantly based in Dublin, Ireland. The successful candidate will be expected to work on programmes/projects across Germany and Ireland. **What you will help us to achieve** *The main duties and responsibilities included in this role:* * Become familiar with CLEs Point of Sales Systems and propose/implement changes as required to the systems. * Create and update technical specification documents for internal \& external use * Elicit, understand and clarify the requirements of the business stakeholders * Translate requirements into business and functional specifications * Work collaboratively with the Lead Analyst based in Ireland * Work with Systems Architects/Analysts to determine potential solutions and test them for both technical and business compatibility **What you will need to be successful in the role** formats * Ability to test and troubleshoot systems software * Understanding of recognised application/service delivery processes and methodology * Excellent communication skills * Self\-motivated with comprehensive documentation which can be understood by Business stakeholders and I.S. alike. * Experience in electronic data exchange * Experience in at least one programming languages with the ability to write pseudo code * Experience working on eBusiness, Web Application and Web Services projects * Understanding of insurance business practices and processes **The Person** *The successful candidate will ideally:* * Be educated to technical degree level, or equivalent experience * Fluent in German and English * Have excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and documentation skills * Have some experience working as Technical Analyst * Have experience with XML, XSD, PDF Editors, MS Office Suite * Be a dynamic individual who is a motivated self\-starter and excellent team player * Have professional communication skills with a ‘can do’ attitude * Be customer focused and result driven The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. title: KITCHEN TEAM MEMBER
description: We believe eating well should never mean sacrificing taste. Our kitchen team are at the heart of what we do, passionate about food, obsessed with flavour, and focused on delivering fresh, delicious dishes every day. This is a role for someone who loves being in the kitchen, has great attention to detail, and wants to grow their skills in a fast\-paced environment. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time \& Part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.50–€15\.25 per hour depending on experience and role ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Food Safety \& Compliance – Follow all food safety protocols and ensure everything we serve meets the highest safety standards. * Hygiene Hero – Keep the kitchen spotless and ensure equipment is always sanitised and safe to use. * Allergen Alert – Understand allergens and help ensure every dish is safely prepared for all customers. * Recipe Rundown – Work with prep sheets and help keep the kitchen running smoothly with accurate, consistent prep. * Flavour Focus – Care deeply about taste and presentation. You know great food when you see (and taste) it. * Process Perfection – Know the menu, ingredients and procedures inside out. Follow our SOPs to keep everything efficient and consistent. * Helping Hand – Support the Kitchen Leader with stock checks, rotation, and keeping an eye on kitchen operations. * All Hands on Deck – Ready to jump in when it’s busy, support the service line and work wherever you’re needed most. ****Who You Are**** * Food Enthusiast – You’re passionate about food and get genuinely excited about fresh, seasonal ingredients. * Curious – You enjoy learning new things, ask questions, and want to get better every day. * Innovative Spirit – You love being part of a team that’s always improving and evolving. * Value\-Driven – You bring a strong work ethic and a positive attitude to every shift. * Love for Hospitality – You enjoy the buzz of a restaurant and take pride in great food and great service. * Team Player – Always ready to help your teammates and support the people around you. ****Why Work at Sprout**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities – we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team who genuinely care ****What We’re Looking For**** You don’t need to be a chef, but you do need a passion for food and a commitment to quality. Here’s what we value most: * Love for Food – You care about flavour, freshness, and making food that looks as good as it tastes. * Knife Skills – You know your way around a chopping board (or you’re eager to learn). Perfectly sliced carrots? No problem. * Attention to Detail – From portion sizes to plating, you notice the little things that make a big difference. * Good Level of English – You can follow instructions, communicate with your team, and understand food safety requirements. * Reliable \& Organised – You keep things clean, follow checklists, and stay on top of your prep. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You’re excited to build your skills and grow with the team. If you’re someone who thrives in a team, loves a bit of buzz, and wants to grow in a company that values people and positivity, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Kitchen Porter
description: We're currently recruiting a dedicated **Kitchen Porter** to help ensure the smooth running of operations at Compass Group Ireland on a **full** **time basis** , contracted to **37\.5 hours per week, Monday to Friday, daytime.** As a **Kitchen Porter** , you will use your skills to maintain a high standard of customer care and cleanliness. In return, you will have the chance to progress your career with a company that invests in its people, celebrates individuality, and rewards and recognises employees who go beyond the plate. Could you bring your spark to Compass Group Ireland? Here's what you need to know before applying: **Your Key Responsibilities Will Include** * Ensure the kitchen is kept to a high standard of cleanliness * Assisting, when required, with the loading or unloading of deliveries \& supply vehicles * Following food hygiene, and Health \& Safety guidelines in line with training which is provided **Our Ideal Kitchen Porter Will** * Be adaptable and easily embrace changing priorities * Be a brilliant communicator and easily build relationships * Have experience delivering high quality customer care * Take initiative and make decisions that are right for our customers * Possess the ability to work under pressure * Demonstrate exceptional timekeeping and reliability At Compass Ireland, our people are our business. When you join the Compass Group you will be joining a supportive network of incredible individuals, each united by their passion for going the extra mile for the organisations we partner with. We are building a team who care about providing a great customer experience and want to grow and develop as we do. We are dedicated to investing in every colleague joining us and pride ourselves on being an equal opportunity employer; we celebrate diversity and are committed to building an inclusive environment for all our colleagues. We consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Please review our Privacy Notice for details on how we maintain, protect and why your personal data is collected when you apply for a job with Compass Group. We will not retain any CV's or job applications for longer than necessary. Job Reference: com/2705/70071001/52810150/ROI \#Republic of Ireland Compass Group UK\&I is committed to fostering an environment where every individual can truly be themselves at work and has equal opportunities to advance in their careers. We strive to build a culture that respects and celebrates the unique talents, beliefs, backgrounds, and abilities of all our team members. We want our colleagues to feel valued, empowered to reach their full potential, and to thrive \- because diversity is our strength!

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Life Insurance Apprentice
description: **Who We Are** At Cornmarket, we look after the financial wellbeing of public servants and their families. We’ve been focused on public sector employees for over 50 years, learning how to bring our clients the best financial services – from car, home, health and life insurance, to income protection, retirement planning and beyond. **Role Purpose** We are looking for an Insurance Apprentice to join our Life Division. This is an initial three\-year fixed term earn learn contract, which upon successful completion, you will be offered a permanent opportunity within the business. While on the programme, in addition to gaining on\-the\-job experience, you will complete the finance industry APA and QFA professional qualifications as well as a Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in Insurance Practice (Apprenticeship) which is Level 8 degree awarded by IT Sligo. You will receive a tailored induction plan and will be assigned a business supervisor/ mentor to provide support throughout the scheme. We will work with you to enable you to reach your development goals and aspirations for your career at Cornmarket. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who is looking to carve out a career in financial services. **Accountabilities of the Role** * Making outbound calls and taking calls from members of the public sector unions to arrange appointments with the company’s Consultants * Meeting monthly targets which are built around call volume and appointment scheduling (alongside these targets comes an excellent monthly bonus structure) * Dealing with clients in a professional and timely manner with a customer first approach * Cross\-selling other Cornmarket products and services * Performing tasks in a compliant manner to set procedures and prescribed scripts * Completing various administration duties, such as data entry, logging and scanning * Participating in weekly team meetings * Continuously looking for and suggesting ways to improve existing processes * Process’s new business applications using our Proposal tracking System * Ensure new business applications are compliant with defined checking questions * Provide support to the team that Issue life new business in accordance with the terms given by the underwriter * Process incoming correspondence and documentation effectively and efficiently * Work to meet and exceed targets for both call volume and processing of new business. * Provide support to the team that is responsible for dealing with client queries/requests in a professional and timely manner * Follow set procedures guidelines, identifying when it is necessary to escalate cases for alternative handling or manager’s advice * Communicate with Life offices, customers financial consultants regarding the progress of applications * Comply with risk control and regulatory requirements that are relevant to this role * Maintain commercial awareness relevant to this role **Entry Requirements** * Leaving Certificate Requirements: Prior to 2017: * A minimum of 4 grade Ds at Ordinary Level and 2 Grade C3 at Higher Level * Leaving Certificate subjects must include Mathematics and English or Irish * An E grade in Higher Level Mathematics, and a B2 in Foundation Mathematics is a minimum entry requirement * A minimum of 140 points is required for entry to all Level 8 courses. 2017 onwards: * A minimum of 4 grades 4o6/H7 and 2 grade H5 or higher Leaving Certificate subjects * Leaving Certificate subjects must include Mathematics and English or Irish * With English or Irish with O6/H7 and Mathematics with F2/o6/H7 * A minimum of 140 points is required for entry to all Level 8 courses * For students who are over 23 on January 1st of the year of entry, interviews and other selection tools will be used to assess suitability, replacing the minimum requirements above. * Learners who have a full FETAC level 5 qualification will also be eligible to join the programme. **What We Can Offer You?** * Flexible working from home options as part of our Cornmarket Flex ways of working * Significant investments in your professional development (e.g. insurance qualifications, professional qualifications relevant to your role) * Annual leave (increases based on service) * Flexi leave (option to take additional annual leave) * Attractive flexible pension contribution rates * Health insurance or wellness subsidy * Fully paid family leave types (maternity/paternity/surrogacy) * Performance based bonus/commission * Income protection * Life cover * Discounts on financial products * Comprehensive health wellbeing programme * Employee Assistance Programme * Active Sports Social Club **Behavioural Competencies Required** * Committed to working and studying for a level 8 degree simultaneously * Focused and diligent * Hardworking with a drive for success * Exceptional communication and organisational skills * Strong initiative with ability to manage your own workload **Equal Opportunities Data Privacy Notice** Cornmarket is committed to building an inclusive workplace environment, We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer, striving to create a welcoming environment. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, disability, ethnic background, family status, gender identify or expression, martial status, membership of the Traveller Community, national origin, race, religion, sexual orientation. We are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities in the employment application process. To request an accommodation, please contact our Talent Acquisition Team (jennifer.collins@cornmarket.ieor Suzanne.smith@cornmarket.ie) remove as required To review our data use practices, visit our Recruitment Data Privacy Notice. **Controlled Function** This role is a ‘controlled function’ as defined by the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 Regulations 2011\. Any appointment will be conditional on the company being satisfied that the appointee meets the requirements as set out in the Fitness and Probity standards issued by the Central Bank. This requires the company to complete prescribed due diligence to assess the appointee’s fitness and probity.

title: ML Engineer
description: Job Description : Machine Learning Engineer ****About the Organization**** \- A global quantitative trading firm that combines deep research, cutting\-edge technology, and a collaborative culture. \- We build most of our systems from the ground up, and innovation is at the core of everything we do. \- As a Machine Learning Engineer, you’ll play a critical role in shaping the future of AI at the firm—enabling research at scale, accelerating experimentation, and unlocking opportunities across the organization. ****The Role**** \- A versatile ML Engineer to help build and optimize the core infrastructure that supports AI research. \- Works across the stack—from data processing and training efficiency to low\-level GPU programming and performance tooling. \- Contributes to reliable, scalable, high\-performance systems while enabling researchers to understand and improve their models. \- Ideal for someone comfortable navigating complex systems, collaborating across domains, and bringing a strong engineering mindset. ****What You’ll Do**** \- Design and implement high\-performance data pipelines for processing large\-scale datasets with emphasis on reliability and reproducibility \- Apply state\-of\-the\-art techniques to internal training runs to maximize hardware efficiency \- Implement custom GPU kernels \- Create observability and benchmarking tools to help researchers understand model and training performance \- Build and maintain secure sandboxed execution environments ****What We’re Looking For**** \- Experience with data pipeline development and ETL processes \- Strong systems programming skills and performance optimization \- Strong software engineering skills in Python \- Experience writing and optimizing custom GPU kernels \- Experience with observability, benchmarking, or performance\-focused infrastructure at scale \- Familiarity with AI/ML workloads \- Comfortable working in ambiguous, fast\-evolving environments and collaborating across disciplines ****Why Join Us**** \- Join a global quantitative trading firm that combines deep research, cutting\-edge technology, and a collaborative culture. \- We build most of our systems from the ground up, and innovation is at the core of everything we do. \- As a Machine Learning Engineer, you’ll play a critical role in shaping the future of AI at the firm—enabling research at scale, accelerating experimentation, and unlocking opportunities across the organization.

title: Maintenance Technician
description: **WG** NSTAR is a dynamic and growing company with a global footprint. Primarily focused on the semiconductor industry, we provide a platform for you to expand your career with knowledge gained on the job training, and tailored development. We know we need talented people like you that hold similar values, which is why we do not put limits on learning, development, industry, and personal growth. Start your path to a **WG** NSTAR career today! **The Purpose of the Role** As a Maintenance Technician, you will perform daily maintenance tasks in a semiconductor cleanroom environment while supporting the Customer in key areas through fault finding and diagnosis principles throughout the manufacturing process. Your goal will be to adhere to facility standard operating procedures with a focus on safety and quality. Your success will contribute to the facilities' overall throughput while simultaneously developing your own skills in a coveted and highly technical industry. **Person Specification** * You are electrically and mechanically minded * You have a minimum 2 years hands of maintenance experience in a high\-volume manufacturing environment * A strong team player with ability to work well within a diverse team * Have excellent organisational, time keeping, and communication skills * Ability to adapt to different tasks and to prioritise activities * You are looking for a new challenge and to develop your career **Work Schedule** Compressed Work Week within a 4\-shift cycle, 12\-hour shifts (day \& night including weekends) **Principal Duties And Responsibilities** * Scheduled preventative and un\-scheduled corrective maintenance of Semiconductor manufacturing equipment * Work on electrical and mechanical sub\-assemblies and systems * Accurate reporting of root cause analysis, problem solving, data logging and corrective actions on failure mechanisms of equipment and systems * Accurate reporting of failure mechanisms of equipment and systems whilst following detailed work\-based instructions and procedures * Maintaining cleanroom and company protocols * Ensuring safety and quality standards are maintained * Performing quality checks in line with process specifications * Using MS Office and in\-house computer system as required **Role Requirements** * A technical qualification (NFQ Level 6 or higher) or industrial equivalent experience * Minimum of 2 years' experience as an equipment maintenance technician performing scheduled installs, preventative maintenance, and repairs * Ability to use a variety of hand tools, power tools, electric meters in performing duties * Good working knowledge of Microsoft office * Ability to work in a controlled cleanroom environment, which will involve the wearing of a gown and relevant personal protective equipment * 100% compliance with all safety and protocol specs and regulations **Physical Requirements** * Have strong physical stamina to have the ability to stand, stoop, walk and reach for the duration of a 12\-hour shift (breaks excluded) **Benefits Of Working With WGNSTAR** * Discounted rates of on\-site facilities * Free on\-site car parking * Reward \& Recognition Programme * Employee Assistance Programme * Learning and development * Opportunities for career progression This job description is intended to convey information essential to understanding the scope of the job and the general nature and level of work performed by job holders within this job. This job description is not intended to be an exhaustive list of qualifications, skills, efforts, duties, responsibilities or working conditions associated with the position. WGNSTAR does not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies. Thank you. **Equal Opportunities Employer** **WG** NSTAR is an Equal Opportunity Employer in accordance with The Employment Equality Acts. **WG** NSTAR places strong emphasis on equal employment, encourage diversity in the workplace, and apply an open\-door policy for inclusion of all employees. Our diversity program encourages people from any gender, background, ethnicity, culture, education, and experience to join the company and more importantly build a career through employee development.

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Hollyfort, a private company specialising in public works for Government Bodies, including Healthcare and County Councils, is expanding! We're seeking multiple Maintenance Technicians to join our growing team to work on exciting projects throughout Dublin and Leinster. We’re looking for a practical, hands\-on Maintenance Technician who can handle a wide variety of household repair and improvement jobs. If you’re someone who takes pride in fixing things properly and leaving customers happy, this could be the role for you. **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Visiting homes, commercial properties, and public spaces to carry out maintenance and repairs. * Performing a wide range of tasks, including painting, patch repairs, tiling (preferred but not required), and general upkeep. * Undertaking and resolving maintenance problems to a basic level across all disciplines * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance to ensure properties remain in top condition. * Collaborating with tradespeople, general operatives, and subcontractors to complete projects efficiently. * Keeping accurate records of maintenance checks and documenting all work carried out. * Providing a friendly, reliable service that clients and customers can trust. * Proper and safely use of hand and power tools **What We’re Looking For:** * Versatile practical skills across domestic maintenance and repair work. * Experience with painting and decorating, patch repairs, and general upkeep. * Tiling, carpentry, or minor plumbing skills would be a bonus. * A hands\-on, problem\-solving approach and the ability to handle a variety of tasks. * Reliability, good communication, and pride in delivering quality work. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Valid driving license **Why Join Hollyfort?** * Every day is different – no two projects or jobs are the same. * Excellent opportunity to grow and develop * We value skill, initiative, and the satisfaction of a job well done. * You’ll have the chance to use your versatility to make a real difference for our customers. Benefits: * Company Pension * Company mobile phone * Company events * Bike to Work and Tax Saver Scheme * Employee Assistance Program * Learning and Development * Uniform provided Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Experience: * Maintenance Technician: 1 year (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Safe Pass (preferred) * Manual Handling Certificate (preferred) * B Driving Licence (preferred) Work Location: On the road

title: Marketing Associate
description: Roughly a third of all the food the world produces is thrown away, and after households, the kitchens that feed us in hotels, offices, hospitals and universities are among the biggest wasters of all. A single commercial kitchen can bin tens of thousands of euro of food a year without ever seeing it on a balance sheet. It is one of the largest, most solvable problems in climate today, and almost nobody can measure it. Positive Carbon makes it measurable. Founded in Dublin in 2020, we build AI that sees what a kitchen throws away. Our sensor sits above any bin and uses computer vision, the same technology found in self\-driving cars, to automatically capture the weight, volume and type of every item discarded, with no manual logging by busy kitchen staff. That turns an invisible operational habit into clear, real\-time financial data: what is being wasted, what it costs, and where to stop it. The results are real. Kitchens using Positive Carbon have cut the value of the food they waste by up to 90%, lifting gross profit by 2–8% and returning several times what they spend with us. We work with hospitality groups, universities and workplace caterers across Ireland, the UK and the DACH region And we are still small. That is the opportunity. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. We need a marketer who makes the sale faster, not a brand caretaker. Your first job is to build the assets that help our sales team and partners prove food\-cost leakage, build business cases and turn pilots into rollouts. This is a hands\-on product/partner marketing role — sustainability is a supporting message here, not the headline. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. **Tasks** --------- * Produce case studies that lead with food\-cost outcomes context, contract type, baseline, actions, reduction, financial value, expansion. * Create partner enablement: co\-branded campaign kits, client one\-pagers, QBR materials, discovery aids. * Run focused segment campaigns (workplace, universities, hospitality) and measure campaign\-to\-meeting conversion. **Requirements** ---------------- * Self\-starter (must\-have): you've taken something from nothing to real, a project, a club, a side hustle, a campaign, and you like the kind of role where the path isn't mapped out for you. * Strong writing and the ability to build sales assets that actually get used. * Comfort with ROI and financial messaging. * Case\-study experience and an instinct for proof over polish. **Nice to have** * B2B SaaS or hardware\-plus\-software marketing experience. * Comfort being the first and only marketer in a company. **Benefits** ------------ * **Health Insurance** * **Pension** * **Bike to work** * **25 Days Annual Leave** * **Hybrid Work**

We need a marketer who makes the sale faster, not a brand caretaker. Your first job is to build the assets that help our sales team and partners prove food\-cost leakage, build business cases and turn pilots into rollouts. This is a hands\-on product/partner marketing role — sustainability is a supporting message here, not the headline. Roughly a third of all the food the world produces is thrown away, and after households, the kitchens that feed us in hotels, offices, hospitals and universities are among the biggest wasters of all. A single commercial kitchen can bin tens of thousands of euro of food a year without ever seeing it on a balance sheet. It is one of the largest, most solvable problems in climate today, and almost nobody can measure it. Positive Carbon makes it measurable. Founded in Dublin in 2020, we build AI that sees what a kitchen throws away. Our sensor sits above any bin and uses computer vision, the same technology found in self\-driving cars, to automatically capture the weight, volume and type of every item discarded, with no manual logging by busy kitchen staff. That turns an invisible operational habit into clear, real\-time financial data: what is being wasted, what it costs, and where to stop it. The results are real. Kitchens using Positive Carbon have cut the value of the food they waste by up to 90%, lifting gross profit by 2–8% and returning several times what they spend with us. We work with hospitality groups, universities and workplace caterers across Ireland, the UK and the DACH region And we are still small. That is the opportunity. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. Tasks * Produce case studies that lead with food\-cost outcomes context, contract type, baseline, actions, reduction, financial value, expansion. * Create partner enablement: co\-branded campaign kits, client one\-pagers, QBR materials, discovery aids. * Run focused segment campaigns (workplace, universities, hospitality) and measure campaign\-to\-meeting conversion. Requirements * Self\-starter (must\-have): you've taken something from nothing to real, a project, a club, a side hustle, a campaign, and you like the kind of role where the path isn't mapped out for you. * Strong writing and the ability to build sales assets that actually get used. * Comfort with ROI and financial messaging. * Case\-study experience and an instinct for proof over polish. **Nice to have** * B2B SaaS or hardware\-plus\-software marketing experience. * Comfort being the first and only marketer in a company. Benefits * **Health Insurance** * **Pension** * **Bike to work** * **25 Days Annual Leave** * **Hybrid Work**

title: Network Infrastructure Construction Quality Engineer
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain. You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers. In this role, you will: Work with construction teams to integrate quality assurance into data center network pathway infrastructure construction throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in construction design reviews, page turns, and reviews for construction documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during construction. You'll influence construction managers, general contractors, Colocation providers and relevant contractors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical construction milestone checks for pathway infrastructure. You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from construction teams and internal stakeholders to refine quality processes. You'll verify that pathway infrastructure construction meets installation requirements by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections. You'll document and align on construction standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and construction methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with construction managers, and provide guidelines to general contractors, Colocation providers and contractors to ensure consistent execution across all construction activities. This role matters because construction quality directly impacts data center reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in construction design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on. This is an opportunity to leverage and grow your existing program management, quality assurance and engineering experience in an innovative and highly collaborative environment where the results of your work will directly impact AWS and its customers. Key job responsibilities \- Own Quality Assurance related programs to drive world\-class quality in network infrastructure in the new construction of Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Infra Quality Engineer \- Construction will be obsessed with quality, engineering, and design continuous improvements * Review cable tray designs against Amazon’s Policies and Industry Standards and define acceptance checklists for each build milestone * Partner with Construction Management team and Technical Infrastructure Project Manager to ensure network pathway infrastructures are built according to design documents and build project milestones, document early signals for risks and escalate to respective owners for remediations * Capture requirement variants between approved design and physical installation and collaborate with onsite teams and design teams to close gaps in pathway infrastructure installation methods. * Define and maintain updates of Quality Assurance documents including inspection checklists and installation requirements from design specifications, Quality Assurance standard operating procedures, Global Design Standards documents, Global Installation Best Practice Guidelines, Product Specifications for Inside Plant cable routing, and Quality Issue trackers. * Monitor installations and collect lessons learned on network pathway quality * Define Network Pathway Infrastructure Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support Construction teams and as reference guidelines for vendors. * Define quality and operational metrics related to construction performance, establish measurement frameworks, and analyze data to drive strategic improvements and inform decision\-making * Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to perform site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel A day in the life Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast\-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites. As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related field * Experience carrying new design concepts through exploration, development, and into deployment or mass production * Experience with power management and power monitoring systems * Bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent in Electrical, Mechanical, Structural Engineering or equivalent field * Experience in root cause analysis and error correction, identifying changes to procedures and systems to implement long\-term fixes and avoid repeating issues * 4\+ years of experience in network pathway infrastructure design or installation in data center environments * Experience conducting quality inspections in live data center environments with engagement of contractors, engineers, and stakeholders **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Knowledge of engineering documentation, electrical diagrams and standard operating procedures * Knowledge of building codes and regulations including Life Safety, BOCA, NFPA, NEC, or OSHA * Experience in Data Center Engineering Operations, with a deep understanding of electrical and mechanical data center infrastructure * Experience reading and interpreting construction specifications and drawings for all domains * Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities, or experience in managing and troublshooting network Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. With 120 employees and solid annual growth, the group has established itself as one of the **key players in oyster and mussel brokerage in France and Europe**. **Your role — close to the resource, close to the producers** Reporting to the commercial management based in France, you will be responsible for : * Building and maintaining a **comprehensive mapping of Irish oyster and mussel producers** * Identifying and securing the **finest lots** at the best conditions * Developing sales toward **European markets** from Ireland * Managing and developing your client portfolio in Europe * Building strong, trust\-based relationships with Irish and European producers and clients * Regular travel to France and across Europe **You are the right person if :** You have a **deep knowledge of the Irish shellfish sector** — its producers, its regions, its quality benchmarks. A background in oyster farming, seafood trading or shellfish brokerage in Ireland would be a significant advantage.You are autonomous, rigorous, and genuinely passionate about seafood products and the people who produce them. **French is required** for this role, given the close collaboration with the French commercial team. **Spanish or Italian** are a strong plus for broader European market development. **What we offer** * Irish permanent contract — **flexible location** * Competitive base salary \+ attractive bonus * Regular travel to France and Europe * Direct collaboration with an experienced commercial team in Brittany * An ambitious, growing group with real **career development prospects** Pay: €28,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Onboarding Specialist
description: **Company Description** **About us** Publift is a leading global ad tech company that empowers digital publishers with cutting\-edge technology, tools, and services to maximise programmatic revenue across their advertising inventory. We partner with publishers worldwide to optimise their ad operations and drive sustainable revenue growth through innovative solutions and expert support. Publift is part of the Car Sales Group, and we exhibit the following core values in our day to day: * We're passionate about what we do: Our energy and commitment drive us to deliver great outcomes. * We think differently: Innovation is at our core. We challenge convention and seek better ways to do things. * We've got the courage to try new things: We embrace experimentation and learn from failure. * We collaborate for growth: We believe in the power of teamwork to unlock potential and scale impact. * We have fun, but we get it done: We balance performance with enjoyment, creating a workplace that's both productive and energising. **Role Overview** Revenue is only realised once a publisher is live and performing. The Onboarding Specialist owns that journey — from the moment a deal is signed through to a publisher generating measurable revenue uplift. This isn't a handholding role. It's one of the most commercially consequential positions in the business. You'll be part project manager, part solutions consultant, part programmatic analyst — and occasionally part salesperson, persuading a hesitant publisher to adopt a change the data clearly supports. You'll carry a portfolio of active onboards at any time, and you'll care deeply about the speed and quality at which each one closes out. If you're motivated by winning engagements, moving fast, and seeing a direct line between your work and revenue in the bank, this is the role. **What You'll Do** * Own publisher onboarding end\-to\-end: from kickoff through technical setup, go\-live, and early performance optimisation * Drive each onboard to completion at pace — identifying and clearing blockers, not waiting for them to resolve themselves * Analyse publisher performance data to diagnose issues, identify optimisation opportunities, and frame recommendations in commercial terms the client can act on * Configure and audit ad setups across GAM, header bidding, and Fuse.js — with an eye on yield, not just technical correctness * Educate publishers using data, benchmarks, and comparable site performance to build confidence in recommended changes * Run structured experiments (A/B and multivariant tests) and communicate outcomes clearly — internally and to the client * Coordinate across Sales, FIT, Yield, and Engineering to unblock issues and keep onboards moving * Maintain accurate project records and proactively communicate status — no surprises for the client or the team * Contribute ideas and observations that improve how the team onboards at scale **Who You Are** **Required:** * 3\+ years in a client\-facing delivery role — onboarding, implementation, technical account management, or professional services * Comfortable with data: you can interrogate a dataset, identify what's driving a trend, and present the finding to a client without losing them * Some familiarity with web concepts — you don't need to be a developer, but you need to understand how a webpage works * Strong project management instincts — you track your own work, flag risk early, and close things out * Full work rights in Ireland (Irish/EU citizen or Stamp 4 holder) **Strongly valued:** * Experience in programmatic advertising, ad technology, or digital media — agency, publisher, or platform side * Familiarity with Google Ad Manager (GAM), SSPs, or header bidding concepts * Background in adjacent technical fields where the skills transfer: marketing technology implementation, digital analytics, SaaS onboarding * Commercial instinct — you think about the client's revenue outcome, not just task completion * Competitive streak — you want to close onboards, hit targets, and see the number move **Additional information** **A few other things we want to let you know about...** Publift is an equal opportunity employer who prides themselves on fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We encourage everyone of all ages, genders and backgrounds to apply. If you’re a candidate with a disability or you’ll need some adjustments to be at your best, let us know how we can provide you with additional support. We believe work satisfaction comes from two things: working in an unbelievably great team and solving customer problems that matter. Our flat organisation structure allows us to be nimble and innovative to drive both customer and personal growth. We know how important keeping that balance between work/life is and have a range of flexible working options on offer, including part time. We are open to this conversation during our recruitment process if you want to know more just ask!

title: Operations Engineer, AWS Managed Services
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Amazon has built a reputation for excellence with recent examples of being named \#1 in customer service, \#1 most trusted, and \#2 most innovative. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is carrying on that tradition while leading the world in Cloud technologies. As a member of the AWS Support team you will be at the forefront of this transformational technology assisting a global list of companies that are taking advantage of a growing set of services and features to run their mission\-critical applications. You will work with leading companies in this space and directly with the engineering teams within Amazon developing these new capabilities. Overview The AWS Support team is seeking engineers that enjoy solving problems, working with customers, and have a strong technical background in application management and software development including Chef, Git, SVN, and at least one popular programming language. Do you have experience as a Systems Administrator working with Linux, Unix or Windows, and understand the advantages of working in a highly distributed virtual environment? Can you read and troubleshoot code snippets? If you fit the description, you might be the person we are looking for! You will be surrounded by people who are smart, passionate about cloud computing, and believe that world class support is critical to customer success. Every day will bring new and exciting challenges on the job while you: * Learn and use ground\-breaking technologies * Troubleshoot application deployments, recreate customer issues, build proof of concept applications. Apply advanced troubleshooting techniques to provide unique solutions to our customers' individual needs * Build and maintain CloudFormation templates, Chef cookbooks and shell scripts to automate and deploy AWS resources and configuration changes * Collaborate and help build utilities and tools for internal use that enable you and your fellow AWS Engineers to operate safely at high speed and wide scale. * Leverage your day\-to\-day experiences to provide the voice of the customer to internal AWS teams * Drive customer communication during critical events * Write tutorials, how\-to videos, and other technical articles for the AWS customer community * Work on critical, highly complex customer problems that will span multiple AWS services About the team Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn't followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don't let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that's why customers from the most ambitious AI startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Your peers will work from different countries in EMEA but also globally. Here at AWS, it's in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship \& Career Growth We're continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth's Best Employer. That's why you'll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there's nothing we can't achieve in the cloud.**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent, or experience in technical support * Experience with virtualization, orchestration and cloud computing (eg. Hypervisors, VMware, Xen) * Experience managing full application stacks from the OS up through custom applications * Experience with strong project management skills and the ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Experience with a scripting language such as PowerShell, Python, Bash, Perl, Ruby or similar * Knowledge of networking protocols such as HTTP, DNS and TCP/IP * Experience in a DevOps team, or experience setting up, maintaining and coordinating the automated patch and configuration management of production servers. * Experience with Chef, particularly writing Chef cookbooks to automate deployment and maintenance on Linux or Windows application servers Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
