title: Trainee Dental Nurse / Front Desk
description: **About the Role** We are seeking an enthusiastic, motivated, and patient\-focused individual to join our modern boutique dental practice as a **Trainee Dental Nurse / Front Desk** . This is an exciting opportunity to become part of a supportive and professional dental team, combining clinical dental nursing duties with front\-of\-house and patient care responsibilities. We are also open to applicants interested in a trainee dental nursing role, with a study package available for the right candidate. As a key member of our team, you will help deliver an exceptional patient experience from first contact through to treatment completion, while supporting clinicians with chairside assistance and maintaining the highest standards of patient care and infection control. **Key Responsibilities** **Front Desk \& Patient Experience** * Meet and greet patients, providing a warm and professional welcome. * Manage the appointment diary efficiently, including booking, confirming, and rescheduling appointments. * Answer incoming calls and respond to treatment enquiries, converting enquiries into appointments where appropriate. * Ensure all pre\-appointment documentation is completed and accurately added to patient records. * Provide patients with clear information regarding their appointments and treatment journey. * Respond promptly, sympathetically, and professionally to patient concerns and queries. * Process payments, arrange follow\-up appointments, and request patient feedback and testimonials. * Create and maintain office correspondence, including letters, emails, and memos. * Manage insurance claims, including confirming cover and processing claims. * Liaise with clinicians and team members to ensure a seamless patient experience. * Contribute to quality assurance systems, audits, and team meetings. **Dental Nursing Responsibilities** * Provide chairside assistance to dentists during clinical procedures. * Maintain exemplary standards of cross\-infection prevention and control. * Prepare and sterilise instruments and treatment rooms. * Assist with patient management before, during, and after treatment. * Use dental software systems to maintain accurate patient records. * Support the smooth running of daily clinical operations. * Check patients in and out of the clinic and assist with reception duties as required. **About You** The ideal candidate will: * Be a positive team player with a friendly and professional attitude. * Place patient care and customer service at the centre of everything they do. * Have excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Demonstrate strong organisational and multitasking abilities. * Remain calm and professional under pressure. * Have excellent attention to detail and timekeeping. * Be comfortable working in a busy, fast\-paced environment. * Have strong computer skills. * Have customer\-facing experience. * Have experience using dental software such as Dentally (preferred but not essential). * Have knowledge of dental practice procedures and services or be willing to learn. * Hold a Leaving Certificate (preferred). * Have excellent spoken and written English. **What We Offer** * Competitive hourly rate (€15\.00–€16\.50 per hour). * Study package available for trainee dental nurse candidates. * Overtime pay. * Performance bonus. * Yearly bonus. * Bike to Work Scheme. * Employee discount. * Sick pay. * Ongoing training and development opportunities. * Supportive and friendly team environment. * Modern, fully computerised dental practice equipped with the latest technology. **Working Hours** * Full\-time position. * Flexible shifts and hours, including one evening shift per week. * Occasional overtime and holiday cover may be required. We are expanding and looking for the right candidate to grow with our practice. Every member of our team is highly valued, and we are committed to creating a positive, supportive, and rewarding workplace. Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time Pay: €15\.00\-€16\.60 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee discount * Sick pay Application question(s): * Explain why you are a good fit for this role? Education: * Leaving Certificate (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Trainee Merchandiser
description: Because your perspective shapes trends. Innovate, your way. **Merchandising at Primark** Our merchandising team turn strategic goals into vast opportunities. Here, your insights will maximise our growth as a business and ensure we continue to stay at the forefront in the world of retail fashion. **What You’ll Do As a Trainee Merchandiser** As a Trainee Merchandiser at Primark, you’ll assist the Merchandiser and Assistant Merchandiser in the delivery of our sales, stocks and contribution budgets. You’ll learn about the industry and be given all the tools you need to progress in a Merchandiser role. Primark is a growing business in a competitive industry, with exciting expansion across the UK, Europe, and the US. Your contribution will focus on delivering budgets and line projections, investigating data inaccuracies and forecasting sales. You’ll also have exciting responsibilities relating to products and trends. **What You'll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: * 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. * Flexible Working, opportunity for an early Friday finish, and a subsidised cafeteria. * Primark Perks – Discounts with some local partner business to our offices **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Analytical \- An analytical thinker with an eye for detail and numbers, who also loves working in a team. * Curious \- Investigate and resolve data inaccuracies, report on stock overages and shortages and ensure that current commitment records have been maintained accurately. * Customer focused \- Develop a strong knowledge of our global customer base and use this knowledge to offer outstanding value to our customer, and key recommendations to your team. * Strategically minded \- Act as a self\-starter, working on a range of products trading in multiple regions. As your knowledge of your product and customer grows, you’ll identify opportunities – learning from sales reactions. * Never miss an opportunity \- Use your initiative to identify current and future trading opportunities and propose your recommendations to your team. * Total organisation \- Use your passion for people to build key relationships that will enable you to progress deliveries and shipping documentation, drive stakeholder engagement, and collaborate with your team. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business a over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility requests, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** REF3761N

title: Trainee Optical Assistant
description: ContractTypeAndDuration Full Time, Permanent **The role** ------------ **Specsavers, Maynooth** Trainee Optical Assistant So, you’re a proactive people person, passionate about retail and offering the best customer service? Sounds like you’d be a great fit here. So, if you’ve ever considered a career in optics, this Trainee Optical Assistant role could be perfect for you. As a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t need to have previous experience, you just need to be prepared to create an outstanding store environment for both our customers and our team, making sure every patient receives the best care in our stores. We aren’t looking for people with years of experience and heaps of qualifications, just passionate, hard\-workers ready to confidently welcome and help customers as they walk through our doors. And once you’ve found your feet as a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t just have to stop there. We have plenty of professional training and development in other business areas, as well as the support of experienced Opticians and Technicians supporting you on your journey. So, whatever you want your career to look like, you can bring it to life at Specsavers. **Our store** Based in Manor Mills Shopping Centre, Specsavers Maynooth is a community driven store that actively participates with charities and community initiatives alike. **What’s on Offer?** As well as all the support you need and great training and development opportunities, we have a whole load of benefits on offer for you to enjoy. These include: * Full Time Hours \- 40 \- weekend \& evening hours included * €14\.15 per hour * Bonus and Staff Discounts * Outstanding clinical and professional development opportunities * Regular team events and team building evenings **What we’re looking for?** If this is still sounding like a bit of you, we do need you to have a few skills to get started as a Trainee Optical Assistant. These include: * Passion for providing excellent customer service and making customers’ lives easier * Great teamwork * A good initiative * Confidence in using IT systems * Excellent communication and listening skills * Prepared to step out of your comfort zone and try something new * Able to receive feedback and be open to development, knowing we’ll be there to support and guide you throughout your process (with your openness and our guidance, your opportunities will be endless). **Find out more** If you’ve got all these skills, we’d love for you to apply, but there’s also some desirable experience that could set you apart from the competition if you have it. **This includes** * Previous experience in a fast\-paced customer service environment * Experience in optics or Audiology business * Basic knowledge/experience of optical and/or Audiology terminology. **Checked all the boxes? Now’s the perfect time to apply!** Andrew Hastings Recruiter Email andrew.hastings1@specsavers.com Contact Stay connected Salary: €14\.15 ### **Key information** Location Maynooth * Area of interest: Trainee Optical Assistant * Contract type: Permanent Apply by 9th July

title: Trainee Property Development Coordinator
description: **Job Title: Trainee Property Development Coordinator** **Location: Dublin** **Hours: 37 hours per week** **Reporting to: New Business \& Property Development Manager** **About Us:** Tuath Housing has provided safe, secure and affordable housing since 2006\. A lot has changed over the years, but our values remain the same. In partnership with Key Stakeholders, our main focus is on delivering more quality homes to more people and providing an outstanding Customer experience to our 27,000 residents. Working with Tuath Housing is different because we make a difference. We maintain and develop our team of dedicated, motivated, and authentic people through focused training and career development. If you are driven to make a difference and believe that everybody needs a home, Tuath is waiting to hear from you. **Position Summary:** The Trainee Property Development Coordinator supports the Development Team who are responsible for delivering new housing development opportunities. They provide administration support and maintain relationships with key stakeholders for the delivery of new housing stock. **Qualifications:** Suitable candidates will have a relevant third level qualification in Spatial Planning, Real Estate, or other related discipline. Own vehicle and full clean driving license required. Good understanding of MS Office. **You will:** Identify and respond to new business opportunities, negotiating and ensuring they are commercially viable. These new business opportunities should include social, cost rental and private rental options. Establish and maintain strong relationships with external stakeholders to further the expansion of the Association via new business opportunities. Analyse the need for housing provision in partnership with Local Authorities and prepare project appraisals to include surveys, briefs, valuations, economic investment appraisals, business cases etc. Assist the Development team with submitting funding applications to Local Authorities and the Department of Housing, Planning \& Local Government (DHPLG) Arrange site visits and liaise with external and internal stakeholders for information Assist the Development Manager and Director in the preparation of tender submissions for new business. Assisting with the maintenance of the New Business team's filing system in line with audit and regulatory requirements Assist in the delivery of the Development Programme for Tuath Housing within agreed timescales Provide general administration support to the New Business Team as required **Requirements:** Understanding of development, construction, negotiation, and property acquisitions. Understanding of working with partners and relationship management. Excellent communication, interpersonal and relationship\-building skills. Ability to work effectively as part of a team and independently. Excellent organisational and time\-management skills. To participate on forums/working groups/committees as required. Promote a culture which is supportive of excellent service delivery and meets Tuath Housing's vision, mission, and values. **Working for Tuath Housing:** Attractive salary commensurate with experience 24 days annual leave Service leave Organisational performance bonus Generous pension scheme Sick leave Wellness allowance Hybrid working Bike\-to\-work scheme Flexible working hours Career break Career development program Employee assistance program Linkedin Learning licence Car\-parking on site Sports \& Social Club Work\-life balance package Tuath Housing are accredited with the Bronze Award from Investors for Diversity and are committed to promoting a diverse and respectful workplace reflective of all cultures, nationalities, backgrounds, genders, members of the LGBTQ\+ community and people from traditionally marginalised backgrounds.

title: UI Engineer
description: We’re partnering with a well\-funded early\-stage startup building an AI\-powered operational intelligence platform for the food manufacturing industry. The team is tackling complex forecasting, production planning, and supply chain optimization problems through advanced predictive analytics and modern AI systems. They’re now hiring a Founding Design Engineer to own both product experience and frontend development for a highly interactive B2B platform. What You’ll Do * Lead end\-to\-end UX and frontend development * Build intuitive interfaces for complex forecasting and operational data * Design AI\-native workflows and chatbot experiences * Create interactive dashboards and data visualizations * Collaborate closely with product, research, and engineering teams * Drive rapid iteration using product analytics and user feedback Requirements * Strong experience designing B2B SaaS products * Expert in Figma, React, Next.js, TypeScript, and TailwindCSS * Experience visualizing complex datasets and time\-series data * Familiarity with AI/LLM products and streaming AI interfaces * Startup mindset with strong ownership and execution skills Additional Info * Competitive salary \+ meaningful equity * 35 days paid leave \+ strong benefits package * No visa sponsorship available * Must be authorized to work in Ireland

title: Van Driver/Warehouse Operative

Due to company growth we are seeking to recruit Van Drivers / Warehouse Operatives to join our team. **Duties:** This is a combined role, you will carry out deliveries mainly to construction sites and collections using company van (new vehicle) and assisting with loading/unloading vehicles, stock handling and other relevant warehouse duties while adhering to all health \& safety requirements. Reporting damages, accidents, and issues. **Requirements:** * Full clean driving licence category B * Van driving experience * Warehouse or general operative experience * Forklift driving experience beneficial (we can organise refresher training) * Manual handling (we can organise training) * Fluent English and good communication skills * Strong work ethic and reliability * Ability to lift boxes **Desirable:** * Safepass Pay: €32,000\.00\-€39,000\.00 per year Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * Driving licence category B (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Veterinary Practitioner - Small Animal
description: ### ***Shenick Veterinary Hospital, Skerries*** ### ***Permanent \\u007C Full\-Time*** ### ***Salary up to €60,000 DOE*** **Join a Leading Small Animal Hospital with Real Clinical Opportunity** ----------------------------------------------------------------------- Shenick Veterinary Hospital in Skerries is proud to offer an exciting opportunity for a Veterinary Practitioner to join our well\-established, progressive team on a full\-time, permanent basis. This is more than a typical GP role – it’s your chance to work in a large, modern hospital environment with exceptional facilities, a collaborative team, and the freedom to shape your clinical future. **A Role That Grows With You** ------------------------------ We’re looking for a confident vet with solid first\-opinion experience who is ready to take the next step. This position is ideal for someone who enjoys general practice or those eager to develop a niche clinical focus over time. Whether your interests lie in internal medicine, surgery, or another pathway, you’ll have the support, case exposure and flexibility to progress towards advanced or referral\-level work in the future. Your day\-to\-day will include a mix of consultations, case work\-ups, routine procedures and surgery, with the time and support needed to work cases to a high standard. **A True Hospital Setting** --------------------------- Based primarily in our flagship Skerries hospital, you’ll enjoy access to outstanding facilities, including: * Multiple fully\-equipped operating suites * Separate dog and cat wards * On\-site hydrotherapy pool with a dedicated rehabilitation team * Comprehensive in\-house diagnostics You’ll work alongside a skilled and supportive team of GP vets, experienced nurses, and support staff, with additional collaboration across our branch clinics in Balbriggan and Stamullen. **Work\-Life Balance That Works** --------------------------------- We understand the importance of balance and have created a rota that supports both your professional and personal life: * 40\-hour week * 1 in 3 Saturdays (daytime only) * No out\-of\-hours or on\-call * No bank holidays This structure gives you consistency, predictability, and time to recharge. **Who We’re Looking For** ------------------------- * VCI\-registered Veterinary Surgeon (essential) * Proven experience in small animal first\-opinion practice * Confident with routine consultations, medical work\-ups and standard surgeries * A proactive, team\-oriented approach with a desire to grow clinically *Please note: this role is not suitable for new graduates or those requiring a high level of mentorship, but could suit a capable junior vet ready to step up.* **A Package That Reflects Your Value** -------------------------------------- We offer a competitive salary of up to €60,000 DOE, alongside a comprehensive benefits package: ### **Work\-Life Balance** * 22 days annual leave * Paid birthday leave ### **Wellbeing Support** * Paid maternity and paternity leave * Employee Assistance Programme \& wellbeing resources * Cycle to Work scheme * Paid bereavement leave * Company sick pay ### **Professional Development** * €1,300 CPD allowance \+ 5 paid CPD days * Funding for certificates, diplomas or further qualifications * Clear career progression pathways * Annual Care Fund contribution (€1,100\) ### **Professional Fees** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover included ### **Additional Benefits** * Employer pension contributions * Annual pay reviews * Retail discounts and cashback scheme * Discounted veterinary fees * Flu vaccinations * Access to internal committees and interest groups **Let’s Build Your Future Together** ------------------------------------ If you’re looking for a role where you can develop your skills, explore advanced clinical interests, and be part of a genuinely supportive hospital team, we’d love to hear from you. Come and discover how Shenick Veterinary Hospital can support not just your next role – but your long\-term career. Up to €60,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

The quality, expertise and commitment of our loyal employees has been the foundation of our success and will be the biggest driver of our business in further development. It is this passion about our industries and products which ensures that each of our customers, big and small, receives a bespoke and high\-quality customer experience every time that they deal with IITC. Our business is underpinned by our 5 core values, as how we achieve success is just as important as being successful \-Loyalty \-Quality \-Integrity \-Respect \& Inclusiveness \-Unique customer service. Kildare.** We are looking for people with a proactive attitude and a willingness to learn. IITC will provide full on the job training and development and upskilling opportunities are available within our organisation **Job Description** * Loading/Unloading of vehicles * Adherence to all Health \& Safety Procedures * Moving and Storing products in correct areas * Check in deliveries and highlight issues to your supervisor. * Check orders prior to dispatch and ensure there no shortages, duplicates etc. * Pack orders and wrap for dispatch and highlight issues to your supervisor. * Dealing with colleagues, customers and hauliers in a proactive and engaging way, and with a helpful and pleasant manner * Monitoring and counting stocks * Cleaning and maintaining equipment and work area * Work as part of a team and on your own initiative * Following workplace health and safety procedures. * Other Tasks as assigned by the Manager/Supervisor from time to time **Requirements** * Enjoy a team\-oriented warehouse environment * Previous Warehouse experience * Forklift \& Manual Handling Certification Preferred * Interpersonal and communication skills * Time management and organisation skills * Attentive to detail and quality focused **What we offer** * Training \& Development Opportunities * Free Annual Health Screening * Pension Plan * On Site Parking * EAP * Sports \& Social Club * Bike to Work Scheme * Medical Expenses Reimbursement Our company is committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of age, sexual orientation, gender, pregnancy, religion, nationality, ethnic origin, disability, medical history, skin colour, marital status, genetic information, socio economic or parental status. title: Warehouse Operative
description: **Step into Your Next Role with Skechers** At Skechers, comfort, style, and innovation are at the heart of everything we do, and that starts behind the scenes. We're looking for an energetic and reliable Warehouse Operative to support our fast\-paced retail operations and help keep our stores stocked with the footwear our customers love. **What You'll Be Doing** * Receiving and processing deliveries of Skechers footwear and accessories * Accurately picking, packing, and preparing stock for store replenishment or customer orders * Making sure the right styles and sizes are in the right place, at the right time * Keeping the warehouse organised, tidy, and ready for action * Using stock systems or handheld scanners to manage inventory * Supporting stock counts, transfers, and loss prevention procedures * Working closely with store and warehouse teams during busy trading periods * Following health \& safety guidelines to keep yourself and your team safe **What We're Looking For** * A positive, can\-do attitude and strong work ethic * Great attention to detail (especially with sizes and styles) * Ability to work in a fast\-moving retail environment * Team player who's happy to roll up their sleeves and get stuck in * Comfortable with manual handling and being on your feet * Basic IT skills (training provided) **Experience** * Warehouse, stockroom, or retail experience is a plus \- but not essential * Experience with footwear or apparel stock is an advantage * Full training provided, so enthusiasm matters most **What's in It for You** Be part of a vibrant and diverse community that welcomes everyone with open arms. * Challenge yourself daily and watch your potential soar. * We're all about communication, transparency, trust, and fairness. * You get the chance to join an inclusive culture that's as stylish as our shoes! * Perks and Benefits that Rock: * Competitive base pay to keep you grooving. * Employee Assistance Programme \- we've got your back. * Incentives and rewards that make work even more awesome. * Staff discounts \- because we want you to rock our shoes! * Free shoes \- yes, you read that right! * Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active. * Service anniversary rewards to celebrate your journey with us. * Career development opportunities \- it's all about the climb. * An excellent work culture that's the heartbeat of Skechers. * And so much more!! If you're motivated, hands\-on, and ready to help keep Skechers moving, we'd love to hear from you. **Apply today and take the next step in your journey with Skechers.** *Skechers is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.*

title: Warehouse Operative
description: **Job Title: Warehouse Operative** **Reports To** Warehouse Supervisor / Warehouse Manager **Job Purpose** To support the efficient running of the warehouse by handling goods safely and accurately, managing inbound and outbound stock, and ensuring all transactions are processed correctly. The role includes operating forklifts and ensuring compliance with company procedures, particularly within a medical and surgical equipment environment. **Key Responsibilities** **Goods In \& Receiving** * Receive, unload, and check incoming deliveries against purchase orders and delivery notes * Inspect goods for damage, quality, and accuracy * Accurately record and book in stock using warehouse management systems * Store goods in appropriate locations, following stock rotation principles (e.g. FIFO) **Goods Out \& Dispatch** * Pick, pack, and prepare orders for dispatch in line with customer requirements * Ensure correct products, quantities, and documentation are included with all shipments * Coordinate with carriers for timely collections and deliveries * Maintain high standards of packaging to protect medical and surgical equipment **Stock Control \& Transactions** * Maintain accurate inventory records and carry out system transactions (goods receipt, transfers, dispatch) * Participate in regular stock counts and cycle counts * Report discrepancies and assist in investigations **Forklift \& Equipment Operation** * Safely operate forklifts (e.g. counterbalance/reach) to move and store goods * Conduct pre\-use safety checks on equipment * Ensure compliance with all health and safety regulations during operation **Health, Safety \& Compliance** * Adhere to all health and safety policies and procedures * Follow company guidelines relating to handling medical and surgical equipment * Maintain a clean, safe, and organised warehouse environment * Use appropriate PPE where required **General Duties** * Assist with loading and unloading vehicles * Work collaboratively with team members and other departments * Support continuous improvement initiatives within the warehouse * Undertake additional duties as required by management **Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * Experience working in a warehouse environment * Valid forklift licence (or willingness to obtain) * Understanding of goods in/out processes * Basic IT skills for stock systems and transactions * Good attention to detail and accuracy * Ability to work as part of a team **Desirable** * Experience handling medical or regulated products * Knowledge of warehouse management systems (WMS) * Experience with stock control and inventory management **Qualifications** * Forklift certification (e.g. Counterbalance/Reach) – preferred * Minimum secondary school education (or equivalent)

title: Work Ready Graduate Programme
description: **Learnovate Work Ready Graduate Programme** * Edtech Industry work placement * Training \& One\-to\-one mentoring * Laptop provided * Salary: €38,000 \+ annual leave * Building networks in industry and innovation ecosystems * Duration: Full\-time, 1 year (August 2026 – August 2027\) \*\*\*\* Please note: **Only expressions of interest submitted via the google form link** below will be considered for application to the programme. Please do not apply via any other methods. \*\*\*\* Apply here https://forms.gle/LSFDNoGeXho1asoj9 **About Learnovate** Learnovate is an industry lead research and innovation centre focused on the Future of Work and Learning supported by Enterprise Ireland, the IDA and hosted by Trinity College Dublin. Our team has rich expertise in learning science, technology, user\-centric product design and customer\-centric innovation for over twelve years. We are excited to announce that applications are open for Year 3 of our Work Ready Graduate Programme, which aims to provide opportunities in the learning technology industry. What is the Learnovate Work Ready Graduate Programme? Graduates participate in a year\-long work placement hosted by a partner company and supported by the Learnovate team, employed by Trinity College Dublin. During the year the graduate will work in the company on a project focused on or involving a learning technology solution supporting the learner in either a corporate learning or a formal education environment. Throughout the 12 month placement the graduate is hosted by one of Learnovate’s partner companies and supported by a mentor in Learnovate, along with the following opportunities: * Formal skills training, networking and engagement opportunities * Business skills training for example accredited Project Management \& Scrum * One\-to\-one mentoring with Learnovate senior researcher * Access to the latest knowledge and technologies in regards to the future of work and learning * Hands\-on experience to implement technical solutions that support the learner in work and across a variety of learning contexts * Conference and events attendance **Benefits:** * Industry work placement * Structured training and one\-to\-one mentoring * Laptop provided * Networks in industry and innovation ecosystems * Salary: €38,000 \+ annual leave **Eligibility Criteria:** * Masters Degree is desirable \- a Level 8 degree is acceptable in disciplines relevant to: 1. software development 2. learning sciences 3. education 4. learner experience/user experience design 5. learning technologies * Interest in/focus on 1. Education 2. User and Learner Experience 3. Learning Technologies 4. Instructional Design * Eligible to work in Ireland (any visa required to work must be in place at the time of application) **The Programme includes:** * Formal training will include skills training, networking and engagement opportunities. * Informal training facilitated by the experience of participation in the Learnovate multi\-disciplinary team activities and exposure to the innovation methodologies in use. * Access to the latest knowledge and techniques within the current technical landscape in regards to the future of work and learning. * Thorough grounding and training in modern business and software design best practices * Hands\-on experience to implement technical solutions that support the learner in work and across a variety of learning contexts. If you fulfil the eligibility criteria and would like to be considered for participation in this programme, please fill out the following form for more information. Depending on the placement company, there may be opportunities for hybrid and remote working. \*\*\*\* Please note: Only expressions of interest submitted via the google form link below will be considered for application to the programme. Please do not apply via any other methods. \*\*\*\* Apply here https://forms.gle/LSFDNoGeXho1asoj9 This is a 12 month placement, commencing in August, 2026 For any queries email gradapplications@learnovatecentre.org

title: Workday Business Analyst
description: **We are open to this being fixed\-term employment or day rate contract. The project is for 6 months.** **About the role** The Business Analyst role will support optimisation of our Workday platform and delivery of Workday's Advanced Compensation module through improved processes, configuration, and governance across HR, Reward, and technical teams. This is a 6\-month contract reporting into our HR Master Data Lead. The role can be performed remotely or in one of our office locations (hybrid options available). **Key Responsibilities** * Supporting Workday optimisation initiatives, identifying opportunities to streamline functionality and improve user experience. * Facilitating workshops, process mapping, and requirements gathering with HR, Reward and business stakeholders. * Translating business requirements into Workday functional specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria. * Implementing automated solutions that resolve or reduce manual processes and align with Workday best practice. * Supporting the creation, embedding and maintenance of HR data governance, including data definitions, controls, data quality frameworks and change governance. * Developing and maintaining process documentation, including process flows, SOPs, configuration guides and change logs. * Supporting UAT planning and execution, including test script creation and defect management. **About you** * Proven hands‑on experience with Workday HCM, including configuration, tenant review and troubleshooting. * Experience implementing or enhancing Workday Advanced Compensation module (merit, bonus, stock). * Strong capability in process improvement and understanding end‑to‑end HR process flows. * Skilled at working with technical teams to ensure accurate configuration, integration and reporting outcomes. * Understanding of HR processes, including Core HR, Talent, Compensation and data governance. * Ability to translate business needs into clear functional specifications. * Strong documentation, analytical and problem‑solving skills. **What Success Looks Like** * Optimised Workday processes that deliver a smoother, more intuitive experience for HR and the business. * Well defined, clearly articulated requirements and documentation that support high‑quality system changes. * A successful rollout of Advanced Compensation, with engaged stakeholders and minimal defects. * Strong governance in place, with clear data definitions, quality controls and change processes. * HR teams confident in new processes and functionality due to effective training and knowledge transfer. * Continuous improvement embedded, with feedback loops that drive ongoing Workday enhancements. **About Inizio** At Inizio, we're a global team driven by a shared purpose: to reimagine health. Formed in 2022 through the coming together of Ashfield and Huntsworth, we unite decades of experience with cutting\-edge capabilities to deliver impactful solutions across the commercialization journey \- from discovery to post\-launch. Our global network of over 10,000 experts operates across 50 countries, supporting clients with strategic insight, medical excellence, creative engagement, and real\-world delivery. We work with the world's leading health and life sciences businesses to solve complex challenges and drive meaningful outcomes for our clients and patients. What sets Inizio apart is our people \- and we believe everyone should have the opportunity to thrive. Here, you're encouraged to reimagine your possibilities: to grow, to be curious and to make real change happen. It's a place where your individuality is valued, your development is supported, and your ambition is celebrated. If you're ready to push boundaries and accelerate your career, Inizio is the best place to be. **Our Pledge** At Inizio, we value inclusivity, recognize the power of diversity and inspire the next generation of change\-makers. We are an equal opportunities employer. We believe in creating a dynamic work environment that values diversity, equity and inclusion. We aim to recruit from a diverse slate of candidates and foster an environment that provides the right conditions for long\-term success. We welcome all applications regardless of race, color, religion or belief, gender, gender identity, age, national origin, marital status, military veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation and physical or mental disability. *Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building an inclusive and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.*
